  "old_text": "//! Attribute Aggregation Map for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregations indexed by attribute combinations with\n//! cardinality limits and overflow handling as per OpenTelemetry specification.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const computeAttributeHash = @import(\"attribute_hash.zig\").computeAttributeHash;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation union type that supports all aggregation variants\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(i64),\n    sum_f64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(f64),\n    last_value_i64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(i64),\n    last_value_f64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(f64),\n    histogram_i64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64),\n    histogram_f64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64),\n\n    /// Record a measurement on this aggregation (lock-free)\n    pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: anytype, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        const T = @TypeOf(value);\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*s| if (T == i64) s.add(value),\n            .sum_f64 => |*s| if (T == f64) s.add(value),\n            .last_value_i64 => |*lv| if (T == i64) lv.record(value),\n            .last_value_f64 => |*lv| if (T == f64) lv.record(value),\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| if (T == i64) h.record(value),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| if (T == f64) h.record(value),\n        }\n        _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1c (lock-free)\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up aggregation resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            else => {}, // Other aggregations don't need cleanup\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregations\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]Aggregation,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*Aggregation,\n\n    /// Initialize the attribute aggregation map\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).init(allocator),\n            .aggregation_pool = undefined, // Will be initialized as needed\n            .next_free = 0,\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .cardinality = 0,\n            .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up histogram aggregations that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) *Aggregation {\n        _ = instrument; // Instrument pointer not used in Phase 1b, metadata hash sufficient\n        // Combine instrument metadata hash and attribute hash for unique key\n        const attr_hash = sdk.computeAttributeHash(attributes);\n        const combined_hash = (@as(u128, metadata.metadata_hash) << 64) | attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |agg| {\n            return agg;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n        if (self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY) {\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata);\n        }\n\n        // Get next aggregation from pool\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation based on instrument type\n        agg.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata) catch {\n            // On error, mark this slot as unused and return overflow\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata);\n        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, agg) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            agg.deinit(self.allocator);\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata);\n        };\n\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata) *Aggregation {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *Aggregation = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation\n            overflow_slot.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata) catch {\n                // If we can't create aggregation, create a minimal sum aggregation\n                return self.createMinimalOverflowAggregation();\n            };\n\n            self.overflow_aggregation = overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *Aggregation {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        agg.* = .{\n            .sum_i64 = .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            },\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = agg;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .{\n                .histogram_f64 = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                    self.allocator,\n                    .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                ),\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregations\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n    };\n\n    const attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    };\n\n    const instrument_ptr = @as(*anyopaque, @ptrFromInt(@as(usize, 0x1000)));\n\n    // Get aggregation for first time\n    const agg1 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata);\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(agg1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n    const agg2 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata);\n    try testing.expectEqual(agg1, agg2); // Should be same pointer\n\n    // Different attributes should create new aggregation\n    const attributes2 = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"POST\" } },\n    };\n    const agg3 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes2, metadata);\n    try testing.expect(agg3 != agg1);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 2), map.getCardinality());\n}\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - cardinality limit\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    // Fill up to the limit (using a smaller limit for testing)\n    // Note: In practice, MAX_CARDINALITY is 2000, but for testing we can't easily create that many\n    // This test verifies the logic works\n    try testing.expect(!map.hasExceededLimit());\n}\n",

This module manages aggregation state per reader, allowing multiple readers\n//! to maintain independent aggregation states for the same instruments.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const AttributeAggregationMap = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").AttributeAggregationMap;\n    const Aggregation = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").Aggregation;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation temporality for metric data points\npub const AggregationTemporality = enum {\n    Delta,\n    Cumulative,\n};\n\n/// Function type for selecting aggregation based on instrument type\npub const AggregationSelector = *const fn (instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType;\n\n/// Types of aggregations available\npub const AggregationType = enum {\n    Sum,\n    LastValue,\n    Histogram,\n};\n\n/// Default aggregation selector based on instrument type\npub fn defaultAggregationSelector(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n    return switch (instrument_type) {\n        .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .Sum,\n        .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .LastValue,\n        .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .Histogram,\n    };\n}\n\n/// Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1b: Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\n    aggregations: sdk.AttributeAggregationMap,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access only in Phase 1c (aggregations are lock-free)\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector (determines aggregation type per instrument)\n    aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n\n    // For cumulative temporality, track last collection time\n    last_collection_time_ns: u64,\n\n    /// Initialize reader aggregation state\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n        aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n    ) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = sdk.AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator),\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .temporality = temporality,\n            .aggregation_selector = aggregation_selector,\n            .last_collection_time_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all aggregations and resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Clean up the attribute aggregation map\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a measurement from an instrument (lock-free for aggregation updates)\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        // Phase 1c: Lock only for map access, aggregation updates are lock-free\n        const agg = blk: {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            // Get or create aggregation for this instrument + attribute combination\n            break :blk self.aggregations.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument, attributes, metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        // Record the measurement lock-free (aggregations use atomic operations)\n        switch (value) {\n            .i64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n            .f64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all aggregations (lock-free aggregation access)\n    pub fn collect(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        // Lock only for map iteration, aggregation data access is lock-free\n        var aggregation_list = std.ArrayList(*sdk.Aggregation).init(allocator);\n        defer aggregation_list.deinit();\n\n        // Copy aggregation pointers under lock\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            var iter = self.aggregations.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |entry| {\n                try aggregation_list.append(entry.value_ptr.*);\n            }\n        }\n\n        var metrics_list = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics_list.deinit();\n\n        const current_timestamp = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Process aggregations lock-free (atomic reads)\n        for (aggregation_list.items) |aggregation| {\n            // Create metric data based on aggregation type (lock-free atomic reads)\n            const metric_data = try self.createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n                allocator,\n                aggregation,\n                current_timestamp,\n            );\n\n            if (metric_data) |data| {\n                try metrics_list.append(data);\n            }\n        }\n\n        return metrics_list.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Convert a single aggregation to MetricData\n    fn createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        aggregation: *sdk.Aggregation,\n        timestamp: u64,\n    ) !?sdk.MetricData {\n        _ = self; // Not used in this helper method\n\n        // Create data points array with single point (no attributes for Phase 1b)\n        const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n        errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n\n        // Empty attributes for Phase 1b (attribute support comes later)\n        const empty_attributes = try allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, 0);\n\n        switch (aggregation.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .i64_sum = sum.value.load(.monotonic) },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n   "old_text": "//! Reader Aggregation State for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregation state per reader, allowing multiple readers\n//! to maintain independent aggregation states for the same instruments.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const AttributeAggregationMap = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").AttributeAggregationMap;\n    const Aggregation = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").Aggregation;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation temporality for metric data points\npub const AggregationTemporality = enum {\n    Delta,\n    Cumulative,\n};\n\n/// Function type for selecting aggregation based on instrument type\npub const AggregationSelector = *const fn (instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType;\n\n/// Types of aggregations available\npub const AggregationType = enum {\n    Sum,\n    LastValue,\n    Histogram,\n};\n\n/// Default aggregation selector based on instrument type\npub fn defaultAggregationSelector(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n    return switch (instrument_type) {\n        .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .Sum,\n        .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .LastValue,\n        .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .Histogram,\n    };\n}\n\n/// Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1b: Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\n    aggregations: sdk.AttributeAggregationMap,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access only in Phase 1c (aggregations are lock-free)\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector (determines aggregation type per instrument)\n    aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n\n    // For cumulative temporality, track last collection time\n    last_collection_time_ns: u64,\n\n    /// Initialize reader aggregation state\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n        aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n    ) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = sdk.AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator),\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .temporality = temporality,\n            .aggregation_selector = aggregation_selector,\n            .last_collection_time_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all aggregations and resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Clean up the attribute aggregation map\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a measurement from an instrument (lock-free for aggregation updates)\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        // Phase 1c: Lock only for map access, aggregation updates are lock-free\n        const agg = blk: {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            // Get or create aggregation for this instrument + attribute combination\n            break :blk self.aggregations.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument, attributes, metadata);\n        };\n\n        // Record the measurement lock-free (aggregations use atomic operations)\n        switch (value) {\n            .i64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n            .f64 => |v| agg.record(v, self.allocator),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all aggregations (lock-free aggregation access)\n    pub fn collect(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        // Lock only for map iteration, aggregation data access is lock-free\n        var aggregation_list = std.ArrayList(*sdk.Aggregation).init(allocator);\n        defer aggregation_list.deinit();\n\n        // Copy aggregation pointers under lock\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            var iter = self.aggregations.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |entry| {\n                try aggregation_list.append(entry.value_ptr.*);\n            }\n        }\n\n        var metrics_list = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics_list.deinit();\n\n        const current_timestamp = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Process aggregations lock-free (atomic reads)\n        for (aggregation_list.items) |aggregation| {\n            // Create metric data based on aggregation type (lock-free atomic reads)\n            const metric_data = try self.createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n                allocator,\n                aggregation,\n                current_timestamp,\n            );\n\n            if (metric_data) |data| {\n                try metrics_list.append(data);\n            }\n        }\n\n        return metrics_list.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Convert a single aggregation to MetricData\n    fn createMetricDataFromAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        aggregation: *sdk.Aggregation,\n        timestamp: u64,\n    ) !?sdk.MetricData {\n        _ = self; // Not used in this helper method\n\n        // Create data points array with single point (no attributes for Phase 1b)\n        const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n        errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n\n        // Empty attributes for Phase 1b (attribute support comes later)\n        const empty_attributes = try allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, 0);\n\n        switch (aggregation.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .i64_sum = sum.value.load(.monotonic) },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .sum_f64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .f64_sum = sum.value.load(.monotonic) },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .last_value_i64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.getValue()) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n                        .unit = if (gauge.instrument_unit.len > 0) gauge.instrument_unit else null,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                            .name = \"unknown\",\n                            .version = null,\n                            .schema_url = null,\n                            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                        },\n                        .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                    };\n                } else {\n                    // No value recorded, skip this gauge\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .last_value_f64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.getValue()) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n                        .unit = if (gauge.instrument_unit.len > 0) gauge.instrument_unit else null,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                            .name = \"unknown\",\n                            .version = null,\n                            .schema_url = null,\n                            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                        },\n                        .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                    };\n                } else {\n                    // No value recorded, skip this gauge\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .histogram_i64 => |*hist| {\n                if (hist.getCount() == 0) {\n                    // No data recorded, skip this histogram\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n\n                // Copy atomic bucket counts to regular u64 array\n                const bucket_counts = try allocator.alloc(u64, hist.counts.len);\n                for (hist.counts, 0..) |*atomic_count, i| {\n                    bucket_counts[i] = atomic_count.load(.monotonic);\n                }\n\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = hist.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{\n                        .i64_histogram = .{\n                            .count = hist.getCount(),\n                            .sum = hist.getSum(),\n                            .min = hist.getMin(),\n                            .max = hist.getMax(),\n                            .boundaries = hist.boundaries,\n                            .bucket_counts = bucket_counts,\n                        },\n                    },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = hist.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (hist.instrument_unit.len > 0) hist.instrument_unit else {

  "new_text": "//! Attribute Aggregation Map for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregations indexed by attribute combinations with\n//! cardinality limits and overflow handling as per OpenTelemetry specification.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const computeAttributeHash = @import(\"attribute_hash.zig\").computeAttributeHash;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation union type that supports all aggregation variants\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(i64),\n    sum_f64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(f64),\n    last_value_i64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(i64),\n    last_value_f64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(f64),\n    histogram_i64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64),\n    histogram_f64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64),\n\n    /// Record a measurement on this aggregation (lock-free)\n    pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: anytype, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        const T = @TypeOf(value);\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*s| if (T == i64) s.add(value),\n            .sum_f64 => |*s| if (T == f64) s.add(value),\n            .last_value_i64 => |*lv| if (T == i64) lv.record(value),\n            .last_value_f64 => |*lv| if (T == f64) lv.record(value),\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| if (T == i64) h.record(value),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| if (T == f64) h.record(value),\n        }\n        _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1c (lock-free)\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up aggregation resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            else => {}, // Other aggregations don't need cleanup\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregations\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]Aggregation,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*Aggregation,\n\n    /// Initialize the attribute aggregation map\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).init(allocator),\n            .aggregation_pool = undefined, // Will be initialized as needed\n            .next_free = 0,\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .cardinality = 0,\n            .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up histogram aggregations that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n    ) *Aggregation {\n        _ = instrument; // sdk.computeAttributeHash(attributes);\n        const combined_hash = (@as(u128, metadata.metadata_hash) << 64) | attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |agg| {\n            return agg;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n        if (self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY) {\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        }\n\n        // Get next aggregation from pool\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation based on instrument type and value type\n        agg.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n            // On error, mark this slot as unused and return overflow\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata);\n        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, agg) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            agg.deinit(self.allocator);\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata);\n        };\n\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata) *Aggregation {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *Aggregation = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation\n            overflow_slot.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                // If we can't create aggregation, create a minimal sum aggregation\n                return self.createMinimalOverflowAggregation();\n            };\n\n            self.overflow_aggregation = overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *Aggregation {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        agg.* = .{\n            .sum_i64 = .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            },\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = agg;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (value) {\n                .i64 => .{\n                    .histogram_i64 = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    ),\n                },\n                .f64 => .{\n                    .histogram_f64 = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    ),\n                },\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregations\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n 0x1000)));\n\n    // Get aggregation for first time\n    const agg1 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(agg1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n    const agg2 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 This module manages aggregations indexed by attribute combinations with\n//! cardinality limits and overflow handling as per OpenTelemetry specification.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const computeAttributeHash = @import(\"attribute_hash.zig\").computeAttributeHash;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation union type that supports all aggregation variants\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(i64),\n    sum_f64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(f64),\n    last_value_i64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(i64),\n    last_value_f64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(f64),\n    histogram_i64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64),\n    histogram_f64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64),\n\n    /// Record a measurement on this aggregation (lock-free)\n    pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: anytype, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        const T = @TypeOf(value);\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*s| if (T == i64) s.add(value),\n            .sum_f64 => |*s| if (T == f64) s.add(value),\n            .last_value_i64 => |*lv| if (T == i64) lv.record(value),\n            .last_value_f64 => |*lv| if (T == f64) lv.record(value),\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| if (T == i64) h.record(value),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| if (T == f64) h.record(value),\n        }\n        _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1c (lock-free)\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up aggregation resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            else => {}, // Other aggregations don't need cleanup\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregations\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]Aggregation,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*Aggregation,\n\n    /// .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up histogram aggregations that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n    ) *Aggregation {\n        _ = instrument; // Instrument pointer not used in Phase 1b, metadata hash sufficient\n        // Combine attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |agg| {\n            return agg;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation based on instrument type and value type\n        agg.* =         return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata);\n        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, agg) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            agg.deinit(self.allocator);\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata);\n        };\n\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata) *Aggregation {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *Aggregation = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n @import(\"attribute_hash.zig\").computeAttributeHash;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation union type that supports all aggregation variants\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: .last_value_f64 => |*lv| if (T == f64) lv.record(value),\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| if (T == i64) h.record(value),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| if (T == f64) h.record(value),\n        }\n        _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1c (lock-free)\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up aggregation resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            else => {}, // Other aggregations don't need cleanup\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregations\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]Aggregation,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*Aggregation,\n\n    /// Initialize the attribute aggregation map\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).init(allocator),\n            .aggregation_pool = undefined, // Will be initialized as needed\n            .next_free = 0,\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .cardinality = 0,\n            .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up histogram aggregations that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n    ) *Aggregation {\n        _ = instrument; // Instrument pointer not used in Phase 1b, metadata hash sufficient\n        // Combine instrument metadata hash and attribute hash for unique key\n        const attr_hash = sdk.computeAttributeHash(attributes);\n        const combined_hash = (@as(u128, metadata.metadata_hash) << 64) | attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |agg| {\n            return agg;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n        if (self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY) {\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        }\n\n        // Get next aggregation from pool\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation based on instrument type and value type\n        agg.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n            //        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, agg) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            agg.deinit(self.allocator);\n create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) *Aggregation {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *Aggregation = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation\n            overflow_slot.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                // If we can't create aggregation, create a minimal sum aggregation\n                return self.createMinimalOverflowAggregation();\n            };\n\n            self.overflow_aggregation = overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *Aggregation {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        agg.* = .{\n            .sum_i64 = .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            },\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = agg;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return agg;\n \"bytes\", null);\n    const gauge_after = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.after\", \"Test gauge\", \"items\", null);\n    const histogram_after = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.after\", \"Test histogram\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    // All instruments created after shutdown should be noop\n    try testing.expect(counter_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(updown_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(gauge_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(histogram_after == .noop);\n\n    // Verify noop instruments have the correct names\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.counter.after\", counter_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.updown.after\", updown_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.gauge.after\", gauge_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.histogram.after\", histogram_after.noop);\n}\n\ntest \"PeriodicReader with multiple instruments\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n    // Processor takes ownership of this memory\n\n    // Create periodic reader with 100ms interval\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.PeriodicReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.PeriodicReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter(), 100);\n\n    // Register reader (provider takes ownership)\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Get meter\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create regular instruments\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const gauge = try meter.createGauge(f64, \"test.gauge\", \"Test gauge\", \"ratio\", null);\n    const histogram = try meter.createHistogram(i64, \"test.histogram\", \"Test histogram\", \"count\", null);\n\n    // Create observable instruments\n    const observable_counter = try meter.createObservableCounter(i64, \"test.observable.counter\", \"Test observable counter\", \"requests\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_gauge = try meter.createObservableGauge(f64, \"test.observable.gauge\", \"Test observable gauge\", \"ratio\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_updown = try meter.createObservableUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.observable.updown\", \"Test observable updown\", \"items\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n\n    // Record some data with regular instruments\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge.record(ctx, 0.75, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 25, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Setup callbacks for observable instruments\n    const counter_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(42, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const gauge_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(f64)) void {\n            result.observe(0.95, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const updown_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(100, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n\n    // Register the callbacks\n    const counter_handle = try observable_counter.registerCallbackNoState(counter_callback);\n    const gauge_handle = try observable_gauge.registerCallbackNoState(gauge_callback);\n    const updown_handle = try observable_updown.registerCallbackNoState(updown_callback);\n\n    defer {\n        counter_handle.unregister();\n        gauge_handle.unregister();\n        updown_handle.unregister();\n    }\n\n    // Wait for periodic collection to happen (simulate time passing)\n    std.time.sleep(200 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n\n    // Force a final collection\n    reader.collect();\n\n    // Verify metrics were exported\n    try testing.expect(mock_exporter.metricCount() > 0);\n    std.debug.print(\"\\nDEBUG: Total metrics exported: {}\\n\", .{mock_exporter.metricCount()});\n\n    // Check that we have the expected metric types\n    var found_counter = false;\n    var found_gauge = false;\n    var found_histogram = false;\n    var found_observable_counter = false;\n    var found_observable_gauge = false;\n    var found_observable_updown = false;\n\n    for (0..mock_exporter.metricCount()) |i| {\n        if (mock_exporter.getMetric(i)) |metric| {\n            std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: Metric {}: name='{}', type={}, data_points={}\\n\", .{ i, metric.name, metric.type, metric.data_points.len });\n            if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.counter\")) {\n                found_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.gauge\")) {\n                found_gauge = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.gauge, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.histogram\")) {\n                found_histogram = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.histogram, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.counter\")) {\n                found_observable_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.gauge\")) {\n                found_observable_gauge = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.gauge, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.updown\")) {\n                found_observable_updown = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: found_counter={}, found_gauge={}, found_histogram={}\\n\", .{ found_counter, found_gauge, found_histogram });\n\n    try testing.expect(found_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_gauge);\n    try testing.expect(found_histogram);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_gauge);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_updown);\n}\n",
  "old_text": "const std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const ManualReader = @import(\"manual_reader.zig\").ManualReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MeterProvider = @import(\"meter_provider.zig\").MeterProvider;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricType = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricType;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const PeriodicReader = @import(\"periodic_reader.zig\").PeriodicReader;\n    const ResourceBuilder = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").ResourceBuilder;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n};\n\n// Import test dependencies\nconst MockMetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MockMetricExporter;\n\n// Helper function to cleanup MetricData memory\nfn cleanupMetrics(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, metrics: []sdk.MetricData) void {\n    for (metrics) |metric| {\n        // Free histogram bucket_counts if present (must do this before freeing data_points)\n        for (metric.data_points) |data_point| {\n            switch (data_point.value) {\n                .i64_histogram => |hist| allocator.free(hist.bucket_counts),\n                .f64_histogram => |hist| allocator.free(hist.bucket_counts),\n                else => {},\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Free data_points array\n        allocator.free(metric.data_points);\n    }\n    allocator.free(metrics);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider lifecycle\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    // Create resource\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    // Create provider (takes ownership of resource)\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    try testing.expect(provider.readers.items.len == 0);\n    try testing.expect(provider.cache.count() == 0);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider meter caching\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope1 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const scope2 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\"); // Same\n    const scope3 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"other.meter\", \"1.0.0\"); // Different\n\n    const meter1 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope1);\n    const meter2 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope2);\n    const meter3 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope3);\n\n    // Same scope should return same meter instance\n    try testing.expect(meter1.bridge.meter_ptr == meter2.bridge.meter_ptr);\n    try testing.expect(meter1.bridge.meter_ptr != meter3.bridge.meter_ptr);\n\n    // Verify cache contains 2 unique entries\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(u32, 2), provider.cache.count());\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider processor registration\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    try @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineBuilder(*sdk.MeterProvider).init(&provider)\n        .with(sdk.ManualReader.PipelineStep.init({}).flowTo(MockMetricExporter.PipelineStep.init({})))\n        .done();\n\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), provider.readers.items.len);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter instrument creation and data collection\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create various instrument types\n    const counter_i64 = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.i64\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const counter_f64 = try meter.createCounter(f64, \"test.counter.f64\", \"Test counter\", \"seconds\", null);\n    const updown_i64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.updown.i64\", \"Test updown\", \"connections\", null);\n    const gauge_i64 = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.i64\", \"Test gauge\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const histogram_f64 = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.f64\", \"Test histogram\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    // Record some measurements\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_f64.add(ctx, 3.14, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, -2, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge_i64.record(ctx, 42, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 15.5, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1 - Collection now happens at reader level, fix in Phase 1b\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance for direct collectMetrics call\n    // const basic_meter: *sdk.Meter = @ptrCast(@alignCast(meter.bridge.meter_ptr));\n\n    // // Collect metrics directly\n    // const metrics = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer cleanupMetrics(allocator, metrics);\n\n    // // We should have metrics for each instrument that recorded data\n    // // Note: Empty instruments might be skipped, so we check for >= expected minimums\n    // try testing.expect(metrics.len >= 5);\n\n    // Temporary: Just verify instruments were created successfully\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable metric verification once collection is implemented\n    // // Verify each metric has proper structure\n    // for (metrics) |metric| {\n    //     try testing.expect(metric.name.len > 0);\n    //     try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n    //     try testing.expect(api.InstrumentationScope.eql(metric.scope, scope));\n\n    //     // Test attributes are empty (MVP state)\n    //     // TODO: This test should break when attributes are implemented\n    //     for (metric.data_points) |data_point| {\n    //         try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 0), data_point.attributes.len);\n    //     }\n    // }\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter data collection through processor pipeline\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n    // Processor takes ownership of this memory\n\n    // Create processor (heap-allocated)\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.ManualReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.ManualReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter());\n\n    // Register reader (provider takes ownership)\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Get meter\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create instruments and record data\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"http.requests\", \"HTTP requests\", \"requests\", null);\n    const histogram = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"http.duration\", \"HTTP duration\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    counter.add(ctx, 3, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 12.5, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Force collection via processor (pull model)\n    reader.collect();\n\n    // Verify metrics were exported\n    try testing.expect(mock_exporter.metricCount() > 0);\n\n    // Check that we have the expected metric types\n    var found_counter = false;\n    var found_histogram = false;\n\n    for (0..mock_exporter.metricCount()) |i| {\n        if (mock_exporter.getMetric(i)) |metric| {\n            if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"http.requests\")) {\n                found_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"http.duration\")) {\n                found_histogram = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.histogram, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    try testing.expect(found_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_histogram);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter shutdown behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create counter and record data before shutdown\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance for direct access\n    const basic_meter: *sdk.Meter = @ptrCast(@alignCast(meter.bridge.meter_ptr));\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable once collection is implemented at reader level\n    // // Verify data exists before shutdown\n    // const metrics_before = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer cleanupMetrics(allocator, metrics_before);\n    // try testing.expect(metrics_before.len > 0);\n\n    // Shutdown the meter\n    basic_meter.shutdown();\n    try testing.expect(basic_meter.is_shutdown.load(.unordered));\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable once collection is implemented at reader level\n    // // Test that collectMetrics still works after shutdown (data preserved)\n    // const metrics_after = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer cleanupMetrics(allocator, metrics_after);\n    // try testing.expect(metrics_after.len > 0);\n\n    // TODO: The following test should work when shutdown behavior is fully implemented\n    // Currently commented out as it may not be implemented yet\n    //\n    // // Try to record new data after shutdown - should be ignored\n    // counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    //\n    // // Verify the counter value didn't change\n    // const metrics_final = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer allocator.free(metrics_final);\n    // // Should still have same data as before the post-shutdown recording\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider flush behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.ManualReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.ManualReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter());\n\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Test successful flush\n    const result = provider.forceFlush(1000);\n    try testing.expectEqual(api.common.FlushResult.success, result);\n\n    // Test flush with failure\n    mock_exporter.flush_result = .failure;\n    const result2 = provider.forceFlush(1000);\n    try testing.expectEqual(api.common.FlushResult.failure, result2);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter comprehensive instrument test with attributes\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Test custom histogram boundaries (for future use)\n    _ = [_]f64{ 0.0, 1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 50.0 };\n\n    // Create all instrument types with different value types\n    const counter_i64 = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.i64\", \"Test i64 counter\", \"ops\", null);\n    const counter_f64 = try meter.createCounter(f64, \"test.counter.f64\", \"Test f64 counter\", \"seconds\", null);\n    const updown_i64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.updown.i64\", \"Test i64 updown\", \"items\", null);\n    const updown_f64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(f64, \"test.updown.f64\", \"Test f64 updown\", \"temperature\", null);\n    const gauge_i64 = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.i64\", \"Test i64 gauge\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const gauge_f64 = try meter.createGauge(f64, \"test.gauge.f64\", \"Test f64 gauge\", \"ratio\", null);\n    const histogram_i64 = try meter.createHistogram(i64, \"test.histogram.i64\", \"Test i64 histogram\", \"count\", null);\n    const histogram_f64 = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.f64\", \"Test f64 histogram\", \"latency\", null);\n\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    // TODO: Test with attributes when attribute support is implemented\n    // This test should break when attributes are added to remind us to update it\n    // const attributes = [_]AttributeKeyValue{\n    //     .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    //     .{ .key = \"status\", .value = .{ .int = 200 } },\n    // };\n\n    // Record various measurements\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 25, &empty_attributes); // Total: 40\n    counter_f64.add(ctx, 3.14, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_f64.add(ctx, 2.86, &empty_attributes); // Total: 6.0\n\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, -3, &empty_attributes); // Total: 7\n    updown_f64.add(ctx, 5.5, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_f64.add(ctx, -1.5, &empty_attributes); // Total: 4.0\n\n    gauge_i64.record(ctx, 1024, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge_i64.record(ctx, 2048, &empty_attributes); // Last: 2048\n    gauge_f64.record(ctx, 0.85, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge_f64.record(ctx, 0.92, &empty_attributes); // Last: 0.92\n\n    // Test histogram with various values to hit different buckets\n    histogram_i64.record(ctx, 2, &empty_attributes); // bucket 1 (1-5)\n    histogram_i64.record(ctx, 7, &empty_attributes); // bucket 2 (5-10)\n    histogram_i64.record(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes); // bucket 3 (10-50)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 0.5, &empty_attributes); // bucket 0 (0-1)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 3.2, &empty_attributes); // bucket 1 (1-5)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes); // bucket 3 (10-50)\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable once collection is implemented at reader level\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance for direct collectMetrics call\n    // const basic_meter:
