= overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *Aggregation {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        agg.* = .{\n            .sum_i64 = .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            },\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = agg;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (value) {\n                .i64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_i64 = hist };\n                },\n                .f64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_f64 = hist };\n                },\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregations\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n    };\n\n    const attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    };\n\n    const instrument_ptr = @as(*anyopaque, @ptrFromInt(@as(usize, 0x1000)));\n\n    // Get aggregation for first time\n    const agg1 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(agg1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n    const agg2 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expectEqual(agg1, agg2); // Should be same pointer\n\n    // Different attributes should create new aggregation\n    const attributes2 = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"POST\" } },\n    };\n    const agg3 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes2, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(agg3 != agg1);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 2), map.getCardinality());\n}\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - cardinality limit\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    // Fill up to the limit (using a smaller limit for testing)\n    // Note: In practice, MAX_CARDINALITY is 2000, but for testing we can't easily create that many\n    // This test verifies the logic works\n    try testing.expect(!map.hasExceededLimit());\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! Attribute Aggregation Map for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregations indexed by attribute combinations with\n//! cardinality limits and overflow handling as per OpenTelemetry specification.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const computeAttributeHash = @import(\"attribute_hash.zig\").computeAttributeHash;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation union type that supports all aggregation variants\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(i64),\n    sum_f64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(f64),\n    last_value_i64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(i64),\n    last_value_f64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(f64),\n    histogram_i64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64),\n    histogram_f64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64),\n\n    /// Record a measurement on this aggregation (lock-free)\n    pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: anytype, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        const T = @TypeOf(value);\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*s| if (T == i64) s.add(value),\n            .sum_f64 => |*s| if (T == f64) s.add(value),\n            .last_value_i64 => |*lv| if (T == i64) lv.record(value),\n            .last_value_f64 => |*lv| if (T == f64) lv.record(value),\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| if (T == i64) h.record(value),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| if (T == f64) h.record(value),\n        }\n        _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1c (lock-free)\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up aggregation resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            else => {}, // Other aggregations don't need cleanup\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregations\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]Aggregation,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*Aggregation,\n\n    /// Initialize the attribute aggregation map\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).init(allocator),\n            .aggregation_pool = undefined, // Will be initialized as needed\n            .next_free = 0,\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .cardinality = 0,\n            .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up histogram aggregations that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n    ) *Aggregation {\n        _ = instrument; // Instrument pointer not used in Phase 1b, metadata hash sufficient\n        // Combine instrument metadata hash and attribute hash for unique key\n        const attr_hash = sdk.computeAttributeHash(attributes);\n        const combined_hash = (@as(u128, metadata.metadata_hash) << 64) | attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |agg| {\n            return agg;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n        if (self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY) {\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        }\n\n        // Get next aggregation from pool\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation based on instrument type and value type\n        agg.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n            // On error, mark this slot as unused and return overflow\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, agg) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            agg.deinit(self.allocator);\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) *Aggregation {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *Aggregation = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation\n            overflow_slot.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                // If we can't create aggregation, create a minimal sum aggregation\n                return self.createMinimalOverflowAggregation();\n            };\n\n            self.overflow_aggregation = overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *Aggregation {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        agg.* = .{\n            .sum_i64 = .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            },\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = agg;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (value) {\n                .i64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_i64 = hist };\n                },\n                .f64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_f64 = hist };\n                },\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregations\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n    };\n\n    const attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(agg1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n                     hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;
                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;
                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;
                    break :blk .{ .histogram_f64 = hist };
                },
            },
        };
    }

## Agent

I need to update the aggregation creation to support the `drop` aggregation. However, the `drop` aggregation should be determined by the view system, not the instrument type. Let me think about this differently.

The `drop` aggregation is a view-level concept, not an instrument-level one. When a view has `aggregation_override = .drop`, measurements should be ignored at the view application level, not at the aggregation creation level.

Let me focus on adding views to the `MeterProvider` first, then handle the measurement flow through views:

@import(\"view.zig\").View;\n    const ViewApplication = @import(\"view.zig\").ViewApplication;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n};\n\n/// Basic meter provider with caching and configuration\npub const MeterProvider = struct {\n    // internal state fields\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    resource: sdk.Resource,\n    cache: std.HashMapUnmanaged(api.InstrumentationScope, *sdk.Meter, sdk.InstrumentationScopeMapContext, 80),\n    readers: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(sdk.Reader),\n    views: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(sdk.View),\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        resource: sdk.Resource,\n    ) MeterProvider {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .resource = resource,\n            .cache = .empty,\n            .readers = .empty,\n            .views = .empty,\n            .mutex = .{},\n        };\n    }\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *MeterProvider) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Iterate over the meters.\n        var iter = self.cache.iterator();\n        while (iter.next()) |kv| {\n            // Unregister the meter from the processors\n            for (self.readers.items) |*reader| reader.unregisterMeter(kv.value_ptr.*);\n\n            // Clean up the meter.\n            kv.key_ptr.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n            kv.value_ptr.*.deinit();\n            self.allocator.destroy(kv.value_ptr.*);\n        }\n        self.cache.deinit(self.allocator);\n\n        // Iterate over the processors.\n        for (self.readers.items) |reader| {\n            // Clean up the processor.\n            reader.deinit();\n            reader.destroy();\n        }\n        self.readers.deinit(self.allocator);\n\n        // Clean up views\n        self.views.deinit(self.allocator);\n\n        // Clean up the resource.\n        self.resource.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n defined method to get a meter.\n    ///\n    /// The provided scope is copied internally.\n    pub fn getMeterWithScope(self: *MeterProvider, scope: api.InstrumentationScope) !api.metrics.Meter {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Check cache first\n        if (self.cache.get(scope)) |meter| {\n            return meter.meter();\n        }\n\n        // Create a locally owned Scope.\n        const owned_scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initOwned(self.allocator, scope);\n        errdefer owned_scope.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n\n        // Create new SDK meter\n        const sdk_meter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(sdk_meter);\n\n        sdk_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(self.allocator, owned_scope, self.resource, self);\n\n        // Register the meter with the processor for collection\n        //\n        // Iterating over processors should be thread safe as they can\n        // only be mutated at start-up / single threaded.\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| reader.registerMeter(sdk_meter);\n\n        try self.cache.put(self.allocator, owned_scope, sdk_meter);\n\n        return sdk_meter.meter();\n    }\n\n    /// Interface defined method to force the attached processor to flush.\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.FlushResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| {\n            reader.collect();\n            const flush_result = reader.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            switch (flush_result) {\n                .success => {},\n                .failure => return .failure,\n                .timeout => return .timeout,\n            }\n        }\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // The mutex block is distinct because the mutex must be released before\n        // forceFlush can be called.\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Flag each meter as shutdown to stop collection and instrument creation\n            var iter = self.cache.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |kv| {\n                kv.value_ptr.*.shutdown();\n            }\n        }\n        const flush_result = self.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n        return switch (flush_result) {\n            .success => .success,\n            .failure => .failure,\n            .timeout => .timeout,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Attach a processor to this provider.\n    ///\n    /// This method is not thread-safe and should only be called during initialization.\n    pub fn registerProcessor(self: *MeterProvider, processor: sdk.Reader) !void {\n        try self.readers.append(self.allocator, processor);\n    }\n\n    /// Convert the provider into an API interface.\n    pub fn meterProvider(self: *MeterProvider) api.metrics.MeterProvider {\n        return api.metrics.MeterProvider{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterProviderBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a pipelinebuilder for this provider.\n    pub fn pipelineBuilder(self: *MeterProvider) sdk.PipelineBuilder(*MeterProvider) {\n        return .init(self);\n    }\n};\n",

return matching view applications\n    pub fn applyViews(\n        self: *MeterProvider,\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n        meter_name: []const u8,\n        meter_version: ?[]const u8,\n        meter_schema_url: ?[]const u8,\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    ) ![]sdk.ViewApplication {\n  if (view.instrument_selector.matches(\n                instrument_name,\n                instrument_type,\n                instrument_unit,\n                meter_name,\n                meter_version,\n                meter_schema_url,\n            )) {\n                // Validate aggregation compatibility\n                if (!isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type, view.aggregation_override)) {\n                    api.common.handleError(.{\n                        .component = .meter,\n                        .error_type = .validation,\n                        .message = \"Incompatible aggregation type for instrument\",\n                    });\n                    continue; // Skip this view\n                }\n                \n                const app = sdk.ViewApplication{\n                    .view = view,\n                };\n                try applications.append(app);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no views matched, add default view\n        if (applications.items.len == 0) {\n            try applications.append(.{ .view = sdk.View.default });\n        }\n\n        return try applications.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an aggregation type is compatible with an instrument type\n    fn isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType, aggregation_override: ?@import(\"view.zig\").AggregationType) bool {\n        // Drop aggregation is always compatible\n        if (aggregation_override == .drop) {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        // If no override, always compatible (use default)\n        if (aggregation_override == null) {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        const override_type = aggregation_override.?;\n\n        // Check compatibility based on instrument and aggregation types\n        return switch (instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => \n                override_type == .sum,\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => \n                override_type == .last_value,\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => \n                override_type == .histogram,\n        };\n    }\n};\n</new_text>\n\n</edits>",

Edited otel-zig/src/sdk/metrics/meter_provider.zig:

```diff
@@ -68,6 +68,84 @@
         self.resource.deinitOwned(self.allocator);
     }
 
+    /// Add a view to this provider
+    /// Views are immutable after setupGlobalProvider is called
+    pub fn addView(self: *MeterProvider, view: sdk.View) !void {
+        self.mutex.lock();
+        defer self.mutex.unlock();
+
+        try self.views.append(self.allocator, view);
+    }
+
+    /// Apply all registered views to an instrument, returning view applications
+    /// If no views match, returns a single default view application
+    pub fn applyViews(
+        self: *MeterProvider,
+        instrument_name: []const u8,
+        instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType,
+        instrument_unit: []const u8,
+        instrument_description: ?[]const u8,
+        meter_name: []const u8,
+        meter_version: ?[]const u8,
+        meter_schema_url: ?[]const u8,
+        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,
+    ) ![]sdk.ViewApplication {
+        self.mutex.lock();
+        defer self.mutex.unlock();
+
+        var applications = std.ArrayList(sdk.ViewApplication).init(allocator);
+        defer applications.deinit();
+
+        for (self.views.items) |view| {
+            if (view.instrument_selector.matches(
+                instrument_name,
+                instrument_type,
+                instrument_unit,
+                meter_name,
+                meter_version,
+                meter_schema_url,
+            )) {
+                // Validate aggregation compatibility
+                if (view.aggregation_override) |override_type| {
+                    if (!isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type, override_type)) {
+                        api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, "applyViews", "Incompatible aggregation type for instrument", null);
+                        continue; // Skip this view
+                    }
+                }
+
+                const app = sdk.ViewApplication{
+                    .view = view,
+                };
+                try applications.append(app);
+            }
+        }
+
+        // If no views matched, add default view
+        if (applications.items.len == 0) {
+            try applications.append(.{ .view = sdk.View.default });
+        }
+
+        return try applications.toOwnedSlice();
+    }
+
+    /// Check if an aggregation type is compatible with an instrument type
+    fn isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType, aggregation_type: sdk.View.AggregationType) bool {
+        return switch (instrument_type) {
+            .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => switch (aggregation_type) {
+                .sum, .drop => true,
+                else => false,
+            },
+            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => switch (aggregation_type) {
+                .last_value, .drop => true,
+                else => false,
+            },
+            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (aggregation_type) {
+                .histogram, .sum, .drop => true, // Histograms can be aggregated as sums
+                else => false,
+            },
+        };
+    }
+
     /// Destroys the provider instance (assumes deinit() was already called)
     pub fn destroy(self: *MeterProvider) void {
         self.allocator.destroy(self);

applications\n    /// If no views match, returns a single default view application\n    pub fn applyViews(\n        self: *MeterProvider,\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n        instrument_description: ?[]const u8,\n        meter_name: []const u8,\n        meter_version: ?[]const u8,\n        meter_schema_url: ?[]const u8,\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    ) ![]sdk.ViewApplication {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        var applications = std.ArrayList(sdk.ViewApplication).init(allocator);\n        defer applications.deinit();\n\n        for (self.views.items) |view| {\n            if (view.instrument_selector.matches(\n                instrument_name,\n                instrument_type,\n                instrument_unit,\n                meter_name,\n                meter_version,\n                meter_schema_url,\n            )) {\n                // Validate aggregation compatibility\n                if (view.aggregation_override) |override_type| {\n                    if (!isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type, override_type)) {\n                        api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"applyViews\", \"Incompatible aggregation type for instrument\", null);\n                        continue; // Skip this view\n                    }\n                }\n\n                const app = sdk.ViewApplication{\n                    .view = view,\n                };\n                try applications.append(app);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no views matched, add default view\n        if (applications.items.len == 0) {\n            try applications.append(.{ .view = sdk.View.default });\n        }\n\n        return try applications.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an aggregation type is compatible with an instrument type\n    fn isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType, aggregation_type: sdk.View.AggregationType) bool {\n        return switch (instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .sum, .drop => true,\n                else => false,\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .last_value, .drop => true,\n                else => false,\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .histogram, .sum, .drop => true, // Histograms can be aggregated as sums\n                else => false,\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Destroys the provider instance (assumes deinit() was already called)\n    pub fn destroy(self: *MeterProvider) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    /// Interface defined method to get a meter.\n    ///\n    /// The provided scope is copied internally.\n    pub fn getMeterWithScope(self: *MeterProvider, scope: api.InstrumentationScope) !api.metrics.Meter {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Check cache first\n        if (self.cache.get(scope)) |meter| {\n            return meter.meter();\n        }\n\n        // Create a locally owned Scope.\n        const owned_scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initOwned(self.allocator, scope);\n        errdefer owned_scope.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n\n        // Create new SDK meter\n        const sdk_meter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(sdk_meter);\n\n        sdk_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(self.allocator, owned_scope, self.resource, self);\n\n        // Register the meter with the processor for collection\n        //\n        // Iterating over processors should be thread safe as they can\n        // only be mutated at start-up / single threaded.\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| reader.registerMeter(sdk_meter);\n\n        try self.cache.put(self.allocator, owned_scope, sdk_meter);\n\n        return sdk_meter.meter();\n    }\n\n    /// Interface defined method to force the attached processor to flush.\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.FlushResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| {\n            reader.collect();\n            const flush_result = reader.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            switch (flush_result) {\n                .success => {},\n                .failure => return .failure,\n                .timeout => return .timeout,\n            }\n        }\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // The mutex block is distinct because the mutex must be released before\n        // forceFlush can be called.\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Flag each meter as shutdown to stop collection and instrument creation\n            var iter = self.cache.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |kv| {\n                kv.value_ptr.*.shutdown();\n            }\n        }\n        const flush_result = self.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n        return switch (flush_result) {\n            .success => .success,\n            .failure => .failure,\n            .timeout => .timeout,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Attach a processor to this provider.\n    ///\n    /// This method is not thread-safe and should only be called during initialization.\n    pub fn registerProcessor(self: *MeterProvider, processor: sdk.Reader) !void {\n        try self.readers.append(self.allocator, processor);\n    }\n\n    /// Convert the provider into an API interface.\n    pub fn meterProvider(self: *MeterProvider) api.metrics.MeterProvider {\n        return api.metrics.MeterProvider{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterProviderBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a pipelinebuilder for this provider.\n    pub fn pipelineBuilder(self: *MeterProvider) sdk.PipelineBuilder(*MeterProvider) {\n        return .init(self);\n    }\n};\n",

   }\n\n    /// Add a view to this provider\n    /// Views are immutable after setupGlobalProvider is called\n    pub fn addView(self: *MeterProvider, view: sdk.View) !void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        try self.views.append(self.allocator, view);\n    }\n\n    /// Apply all registered views to an instrument, returning view applications\n    /// If no views match, returns a single default view application\n    pub fn applyViews(\n        self: *MeterProvider,\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n        instrument_description: ?[]const u8,\n        meter_name: []const u8,\n        meter_version: ?[]const u8,\n        meter_schema_url: ?[]const u8,\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    ) ![]sdk.ViewApplication {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        var applications = std.ArrayList(sdk.ViewApplication).init(allocator);\n        defer applications.deinit();\n\n        for (self.views.items) |view| {\n            if (view.instrument_selector.matches(\n                instrument_name,\n                instrument_type,\n                instrument_unit,\n                meter_name,\n                meter_version,\n                meter_schema_url,\n            )) {\n                // Validate aggregation compatibility\n                if (view.aggregation_override) |override_type| {\n                    if (!isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type, override_type)) {\n                        api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"applyViews\", \"Incompatible aggregation type for instrument\", null);\n                        continue; // Skip this view\n                    }\n                }\n\n                const app = sdk.ViewApplication{\n                    .view = view,\n                };\n                try applications.append(app);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no views matched, add default view\n        if (applications.items.len == 0) {\n            try applications.append(.{ .view = sdk.View.default });\n        }\n\n        return try applications.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an aggregation type is compatible with an instrument type\n    fn isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType, aggregation_type: sdk.View.AggregationType) bool {\n        return switch (instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .sum, .drop => true,\n                else => false,\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .last_value, .drop => true,\n                else => false,\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .histogram, .sum, .drop => true, // Histograms can be aggregated as sums\n                else => false,\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Destroys the provider instance (assumes deinit() was already called)\n    pub fn destroy(self: *MeterProvider) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    /// Interface defined method to get a meter.\n    ///\n    /// The provided scope is copied internally.\n    pub fn getMeterWithScope(self: *MeterProvider, scope: api.InstrumentationScope) !api.metrics.Meter {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Check cache first\n        if (self.cache.get(scope)) |meter| {\n            return meter.meter();\n        }\n\n        // Create a locally owned Scope.\n        const owned_scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initOwned(self.allocator, scope);\n        errdefer owned_scope.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n\n        // Create new SDK meter\n        const sdk_meter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(sdk_meter);\n\n        sdk_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(self.allocator, owned_scope, self.resource, self);\n\n        // Register the meter with the processor for collection\n        //\n        // Iterating over processors should be thread safe as they can\n        // only be mutated at start-up / single threaded.\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| reader.registerMeter(sdk_meter);\n\n        try self.cache.put(self.allocator, owned_scope, sdk_meter);\n\n        return sdk_meter.meter();\n    }\n\n    /// Interface defined method to force the attached processor to flush.\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.FlushResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| {\n            reader.collect();\n            const flush_result = reader.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            switch (flush_result) {\n                .success => {},\n                .failure => return .failure,\n                .timeout => return .timeout,\n            }\n        }\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // The mutex block is distinct because the mutex must be released before\n        // forceFlush can be called.\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Flag each meter as shutdown to stop collection and instrument creation\n            var iter = self.cache.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |kv| {\n                kv.value_ptr.*.shutdown();\n            }\n        }\n        const flush_result = self.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n        return switch (flush_result) {\n            .success => .success,\n            .failure => .failure,\n            .timeout => .timeout,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Attach a processor to this provider.\n    ///\n    /// This method is not thread-safe and should only be called during initialization.\n    pub fn registerProcessor(self: *MeterProvider, processor: sdk.Reader) !void {\n        try self.readers.append(self.allocator, processor);\n    }\n\n    /// Convert the provider into an API interface.\n    pub fn meterProvider(self: *MeterProvider) api.metrics.MeterProvider {\n        return api.metrics.MeterProvider{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterProviderBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a pipelinebuilder for this provider.\n    pub fn pipelineBuilder(self: *MeterProvider) sdk.PipelineBuilder(*MeterProvider) {\n        return .init(self);\n    }\n};\n",

//! OpenTelemetry AttributeValue - Non-owning, lightweight attribute values
//!
//! ## Memory Management
//! AttributeValue is NON-OWNING - it only holds references to data.

api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"applyViews\", \"Incompatible aggregation type for instrument\", null);\n                        continue; // Skip this view\n                    }\n                }\n\n                const app = sdk.ViewApplication{\n                    .view = view,\n                };\n                try applications.append(app);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no views matched, add default view\n        if (applications.items.len == 0) {\n            try applications.append(.{ .view = sdk.View.default });\n        }\n\n        return try applications.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an aggregation type is compatible with an instrument type\n    fn isAggregationCompatible(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType, aggregation_type: sdk.AggregationType) bool {\n        return switch (instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .sum, .drop => true,\n                else => false,\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .last_value, .drop => true,\n                else => false,\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (aggregation_type) {\n                .histogram, .sum, .drop => true, // Histograms can be aggregated as sums\n                else => false,\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Destroys the provider instance (assumes deinit() was already called)\n    pub fn destroy(self: *MeterProvider) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    /// Interface defined method to get a meter.\n    ///\n    /// The provided scope is copied internally.\n    pub fn getMeterWithScope(self: *MeterProvider, scope: api.InstrumentationScope) !api.metrics.Meter {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Check cache first\n        if (self.cache.get(scope)) |meter| {\n            return meter.meter();\n        }\n\n        // Create a locally owned Scope.\n        const owned_scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initOwned(self.allocator, scope);\n        errdefer owned_scope.deinitOwned(self.allocator);\n\n        // Create new SDK meter\n        const sdk_meter = try self.allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n        errdefer self.allocator.destroy(sdk_meter);\n\n        sdk_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(self.allocator, owned_scope, self.resource, self);\n\n        // Interface defined method to force the attached processor to flush.\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.FlushResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        for (self.readers.items) |*reader| {\n            reader.collect();\n            const flush_result = reader.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            switch (flush_result) {\n                .success => {},\n                .failure => return .failure,\n                .timeout => return .timeout,\n            }\n        }\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *MeterProvider, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // The mutex block is distinct because the mutex must be released before\n        // forceFlush can be called.\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Flag each meter as shutdown to stop collection and instrument creation\n            var iter = self.cache.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |kv| {\n                kv.value_ptr.*.shutdown();\n            }\n        }\n        const flush_result = self.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n provider.\n    ///\n    /// This method is not thread-safe and should only be called during initialization.\n    pub fn registerProcessor(self: *MeterProvider, processor: sdk.Reader) !void {\n        try self.readers.append(self.allocator, processor);\n    }\n\n    /// Convert the provider into an API interface.\n    pub fn meterProvider(self: *MeterProvider) api.metrics.MeterProvider {\n        return api.metrics.MeterProvider{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterProviderBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a pipelinebuilder for this provider.\n    pub fn pipelineBuilder(self:     {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Flag each meter as shutdown to stop collection and instrument creation\n            var iter = return switch (flush_result) {\n            .success => .success,\n            .failure => .failure,\n            .timeout => .timeout,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Attach a processor to this provider.\n    ///\n    /// This method is not thread-safe and should only be called during initialization.\n    pub fn registerProcessor(self: *MeterProvider, processor: sdk.Reader) !void {\n        try self.readers.append(self.allocator, processor);\n    }\n\n    /// Convert the provider into an API interface.\n    pub fn meterProvider(self: *MeterProvider) api.metrics.MeterProvider {\n        return api.metrics.MeterProvider{ .bridge = api.metrics.MeterProviderBridge.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a pipelinebuilder for this provider.\n    pub fn pipelineBuilder(self: *MeterProvider) sdk.PipelineBuilder(*MeterProvider) {\n        return .init(self);\n    }\n};\n",

@import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType;\n\n/// Types of aggregations available\npub const AggregationType = enum {\n    sum,\n    last_value,\n    histogram,\n    drop, // Special case: don't aggregate at all\n};\n\n/// Default aggregation selector based on instrument type\npub fn defaultAggregationSelector(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n    return switch (instrument_type) {\n        .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .sum,\n        .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .last_value,\n        .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .histogram,\n    };\n}\n\n/// Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1b: Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\n    aggregations: sdk.AttributeAggregationMap,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access only in Phase 1c (aggregations are lock-free)\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector (determines aggregation type per instrument)\n    aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n\n    // For cumulative temporality, track last collection time\n    last_collection_time_ns: u64,\n\n    /// Initialize reader aggregation state\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n        aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n    ) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = sdk.AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator),\n self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Clean up the attribute aggregation map\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a measurement from an instrument (lock-free for aggregation updates)\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        // Phase 1c: Lock only for map access, aggregation updates are lock-free\n        const agg = blk: {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            // Get or create aggregation for this instrument + attribute combination\n            break :blk self.aggregations.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument, attributes, metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        // Record the measurement lock-free (aggregations use atomic operations)\n        switch (value) {\n            .i64 => |v| agg.record(v, *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        // Lock only for map iteration, aggregation data access is lock-free\n        var aggregation_list = std.ArrayList(*sdk.Aggregation).init(allocator);\n        defer aggregation_list.deinit();\n\n        // Copy aggregation pointers under lock\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .histogram_f64 => |*hist| {\n                if (hist.getCount() == 0) {\n                    // No data recorded, skip this histogram\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(empty_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n\n                // Copy atomic bucket counts to regular u64 array\n                const bucket_counts = try allocator.alloc(u64, hist.counts.len);\n                for (hist.counts, 0..) |*atomic_count, i| {\n                    bucket_counts[i] = atomic_count.load(.monotonic);\n                }\n\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = hist.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = empty_attributes,\n                    .value = .{\n                        .f64_histogram = .{\n                            .count = hist.getCount(),\n                            .sum = hist.getSum(),\n                            .min = hist.getMin(),\n                            .max = hist.getMax(),\n                            .boundaries = hist.boundaries,\n                            .bucket_counts = bucket_counts,\n                        },\n                    },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = hist.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (hist.instrument_unit.len > 0) hist.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .histogram,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n        }\n    }\n};\n",

Views can filter attributes, rename\n//! instruments, override aggregation types, and create multiple streams from\n//! a single instrument.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n};\n\n/// Types of aggregations available for view override\npub const AggregationType = enum {\n    sum,\n    last_value,\n    histogram,\n    drop, // Special case: don't aggregate at all\n};\n\n/// Instrument selector for matching instruments to views\npub const InstrumentSelector = struct {\n    // All fields are optional (null means match any)\n    name: ?[]const u8 = null, // \"*\" = all, supports basic wildcards\n    type: ?sdk.InstrumentType = null, // Counter, Histogram, etc.\n    unit: ?[]const u8 = null, // Exact match\n    meter_name: ?[]const u8 = null, // Exact match\n    meter_version: ?[]const u8 = null, // Exact match\n    meter_schema_url: ?[]const u8 = null, // Exact match\n\n    /// Check if this selector matches the given instrument metadata\n    pub fn matches(\n        self: *const @This(),\n        instrument_name: []const u8,\n        instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType,\n        instrument_unit: []const u8,\n        meter_name: []const u8,\n        meter_version: ?[]const u8,\n        meter_schema_url: ?[]const u8,\n    ) bool {\n        // Check name (minimal wildcard support per spec requirement)\n        if (self.name) |pattern| {\n            // Support exact match and \"*\" for all\n            if (!std.mem.eql(u8, pattern, \"*\") and\n                !std.mem.eql(u8, pattern, instrument_name))\n            {\n                return false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check type\n        if (self.type) |t| {\n            if (t != instrument_type) return false;\n        }\n\n        // Check unit\n        if (self.unit) |u| {\n            if (!std.mem.eql(u8, u, instrument_unit)) return false;\n        }\n\n        // Check meter properties\n        if (self.meter_name) |mn| {\n            if (!std.mem.eql(u8, mn, meter_name)) return false;\n        }\n\n        if (self.meter_version) |mv| {\n            const actual_version = meter_version orelse \"\";\n            if (!std.mem.eql(u8, mv, actual_version)) return false;\n        }\n\n        if (self.meter_schema_url) |msu| {\n            const actual_schema_url = meter_schema_url orelse \"\";\n            if (!std.mem.eql(u8, msu, actual_schema_url)) return false;\n        }\n\n        // All specified criteria matched\n        return true;\n    }\n};\n\n/// View configuration that transforms how metrics are collected\npub const View = struct {\n    // Instrument selector (all criteria are additive/AND-ed)\n    instrument_selector: InstrumentSelector,\n\n    // Stream configuration (per OTel spec)\n    name: ?[]const u8 = null, // Override instrument name\n    description: ?[]const u8 = null, // Override instrument description\n    attribute_allowed_keys: ?[]const []const u8 = null, // Allow list: null = keep all, empty = drop all\n    aggregation_override: ?AggregationType = null, // Override instrument's default aggregation\n\n    // Note: Unit is NOT transformable per spec\n\n    /// Default view that matches any instrument and applies no transformations\n    pub const default: View = .{\n        .instrument_selector = .{}, // Matches everything\n    };\n\n    /// Check if this is a drop aggregation view\n    pub fn drops(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.aggregation_override == .drop;\n    }\n\n    /// Get the effective name for this view (original or override)\n    pub fn getName(self: *const @This(), original_name: []const u8) []const u8 {\n        return self.name orelse original_name;\n    }\n\n    /// Get the effective description for this view (original or override)\n    pub fn getDescription(self: *const @This(), original_description: ?[]const u8) ?[]const u8 {\n        return self.description orelse original_description;\n    }\n};\n\n/// Application of a view to an instrument, handling attribute transformation\npub const ViewApplication = struct {\n    view: View,\n\n    /// Check if this application drops measurements\n    pub fn drops(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.view.drops();\n    }\n\n    /// Transform attributes according to view configuration\n    pub fn transformAttributes(\n        self: *const @This(),\n        input: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    ) ![]api.AttributeKeyValue {\n        // If no attribute filtering specified, return copy of input\n        if (self.view.attribute_allowed_keys == null) {\n            const result = try allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, input.len);\n            @memcpy(result, input);\n            return result;\n        }\n\n        const keep_keys = self.view.attribute_allowed_keys.?;\n\n        // Empty allow list means drop all attributes\n        if (keep_keys.len == 0) {\n            return try allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, 0);\n        }\n\n        // Filter to only specified keys\n        var filtered = std.ArrayList(api.AttributeKeyValue).init(allocator);\n        defer filtered.deinit();\n\n        for (input) |attr| {\n            for (keep_keys) |allowed_key| {\n                if (std.mem.eql(u8, attr.key, allowed_key)) {\n                    try filtered.append(attr);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return try filtered.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Get the effective name for this view application\n    pub fn getName(self: *const @This(), original_name: []const u8) []const u8 {\n        return self.view.getName(original_name);\n    }\n\n    /// Get the effective description for this view application\n    pub fn getDescription(self: *const @This(), original_description: ?[]const u8) ?[]const u8 {\n        return self.view.getDescription(original_description);\n    }\n\n    /// Get the aggregation type for this view (original selector or override)\n    pub fn getAggregationType(self: *const @This(), instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n        if (self.view.aggregation_override) |override_type| {\n            return override_type;\n        }\n\n        // Use default aggregation based on instrument type\n        return switch (instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .sum,\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .last_value,\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .histogram,\n        };\n    }\n};\n\n// Tests\ntest // Should not match different name\n    try testing.expect(!selector.matches(\n        \"http.duration\",\n        .Counter,\n        \"requests\",\n        \"test.meter\",\n        \"1.0.0\",\n        null,\n    ));\n\n    // Should not match different type\n    try testing.expect(!selector.matches(\n        \"http.requests\",\n        .Histogram,\n        \"requests\",\n        \"test.meter\",\n        \"1.0.0\",\n        null,\n    ));\n}\n\ntest \"InstrumentSelector - wildcard matching\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n\n    const selector = InstrumentSelector{\n        .name = \"*\",\n    };\n\n    // Should match any name\n    try testing.expect(selector.matches(\n        \"http.requests\",\n        .Counter,\n        \"requests\",\n        \"test.meter\",\n        \"1.0.0\",\n ## Agent

Great! Now we have **98/99 tests passing**, which is a significant improvement from the 92/93 we had before. The basic view system infrastructure is working. The remaining failure is still about observable instruments, which is a separate issue.

## Agent

I need to modify the instrument's measurement methods to apply views. The current flow is:

```
instrument.add() -> readers.recordMeasurement()
```

I need to change it to:

```
instrument.add() -> apply views -> for each view -> readers.recordMeasurement()
```

Let me modify the `addI64` method to apply views:

**Use Tool: edit_file (toolu_01GPFQyNK9aKs5dPgHaJGfww)**
```json
{
  "display_description": "Update Counter addI64 to apply views before forwarding to readers",
  "path": "otel-zig/src/sdk/metrics/instruments.zig",
  "mode": "edit"
}
```

**Tool Results: toolu_01GPFQyNK9aKs5dPgHaJGfww**

       parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n            config: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregationConfig,\n        ) !@This() {\n            _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1\n            _ = config; // Unused in Phase 1\n\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Histogram,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n    }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Histogram,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, .{ .i64 = value }, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateHistogramValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Histogram.record\", \"Negative value provided\", \"histogram values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Histogram,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, .{ .f64 = value }, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! OpenTelemetry Basic Meter Provider SDK Implementation\n//!\n//! This module provides the basic concrete implementation of MeterProvider for the SDK.\n//! It manages meter lifecycle, caching, and configuration.\n//!\n//! See: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/blob/main/specification/metrics/sdk.md\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n};\n\n/// Standard Counter implementation that forwards measurements to readers\npub fn StandardCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .Counter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(i64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Apply views and forward to readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    // Apply all matching views to this instrument\n                    const view_applications = provider.applyViews(\n                        self.name,\n                        .Counter,\n                        self.unit orelse \"\",\n                        self.description,\n                        self.meter.scope.name,\n                        self.meter.scope.version,\n                        self.meter.scope.schema_url,\n                        provider.allocator,\n                    ) catch &[_]sdk.ViewApplication{.{ .view = sdk.View.default }};\n                    defer provider.allocator.free(view_applications);\n\n                    // Process each view application\n                    for (view_applications) |view_app| {\n                        // Skip drop aggregations\n                        if (view_app.drops()) continue;\n\n                        // Transform attributes according to view\n                        const transformed_attrs = view_app.transformAttributes(\n                            attributes,\n                            provider.allocator,\n                        ) catch attributes; // On error, use original attributes\n                        defer if (transformed_attrs.ptr != attributes.ptr) provider.allocator.free(transformed_attrs);\n\n                        // Create transformed metadata\n                        const transformed_metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                            .name = view_app.getName(self.name),\n                            .description = view_app.getDescription(self.description) orelse \"\",\n                            .unit = self.unit orelse \"\", // Unit not transformable per spec\n                            .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                            .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                            .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                            .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                            .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash, // TODO: Recalculate for transformed metadata\n                        };\n\n                        // Forward to all readers\n                        for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                            reader.recordMeasurement(self, .{ .i64 = value }, transformed_attrs, transformed_metadata);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn addF64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: f64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            if (!api.metrics.validateCounterValue(f64, value)) {\n                api.common.reportValidationError(.meter, \"Counter.add\", \"Negative value provided\", \"counter values must be non-negative\");\n                return; // Return early in validation mode\n            }\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == f64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n                    .name = self.name,\n                    .description = self.description orelse \"\",\n                    .unit = self.unit orelse \"\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .meter_name = self.meter.scope.name,\n                    .meter_version = self.meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                    .meter_schema_url = self.meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n                    .metadata_hash = self.metadata_hash,\n                };\n\n                // Forward to all readers via meter.provider.readers\n                if (self.meter.provider) |provider| {\n                    for (provider.readers.items) |*reader| {\n                        reader.recordMeasurement(self, .{ .f64 = value }, attributes, metadata);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else {\n                unreachable;\n            }\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordI64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: i64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn recordF64(_: *@This(), _: api.Context, _: f64, _: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            unreachable;\n        }\n\n        pub fn enabled(self: *@This()) bool {\n            _ = self;\n            return true;\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n/// Standard UpDownCounter implementation that forwards measurements to readers (allowing negative)\npub fn StandardUpDownCounter(comptime T: type) type {\n    return struct {\n        name: []const u8,\n        description: ?[]const u8,\n        unit: ?[]const u8,\n        meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        metadata_hash: u64,\n\n        pub fn init(\n            name: []const u8,\n            description: ?[]const u8,\n            unit: ?[]const u8,\n            parent_meter: *sdk.Meter,\n        ) !@This() {\n            const metadata_hash = sdk.MetricMetadata.computeHash(\n                name,\n                unit orelse \"\",\n                .UpDownCounter,\n                parent_meter.scope.name,\n                parent_meter.scope.version orelse \"\",\n                parent_meter.scope.schema_url orelse \"\",\n            );\n\n            return .{\n                .name = name,\n                .description = description,\n                .unit = unit,\n                .meter = parent_meter,\n                .metadata_hash = metadata_hash,\n            };\n        }\n\n        pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n            _ = self;\n        }\n\n        pub fn getName(self: *const @This()) []const u8 {\n            return self.name;\n        }\n\n        pub fn addI64(self: *@This(), ctx: api.Context, value: i64, attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) void {\n            _ = ctx;\n\n            if (T == i64) {\n                const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n
