  "new_text": "//! Attribute Aggregation Map for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregations indexed by attribute combinations with\n//! cardinality limits and overflow handling as per OpenTelemetry specification.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const computeAttributeHash = @import(\"attribute_hash.zig\").computeAttributeHash;\n};\n\n/// Entry that combines aggregation with its attributes for export\npub const AttributeAggregationEntry = struct {\n    aggregation: Aggregation,\n    attributes: []api.AttributeKeyValue,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator, // For cleaning up owned attributes\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.aggregation.deinit(self.allocator);\n        // Free owned attributes\n        for (self.attributes) |attr| {\n            if (attr.value == .string) {\n                self.allocator.free(attr.key);\n                self.allocator.free(attr.value.string);\n            } else {\n                self.allocator.free(attr.key);\n            }\n        }\n        if (self.attributes.len > 0) {\n            self.allocator.free(self.attributes);\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Aggregation union type that supports all aggregation variants\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(i64),\n    sum_f64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(f64),\n    last_value_i64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(i64),\n    last_value_f64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(f64),\n    histogram_i64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64),\n    histogram_f64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64),\n    drop: void, // Drop aggregation - ignores all measurements\n\n    /// Record a measurement on this aggregation (lock-free)\n    pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: anytype, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        const T = @TypeOf(value);\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*s| if (T == i64) s.add(value),\n            .sum_f64 => |*s| if (T == f64) s.add(value),\n            .last_value_i64 => |*lv| if (T == i64) lv.record(value),\n            .last_value_f64 => |*lv| if (T == f64) lv.record(value),\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| if (T == i64) h.record(value),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| if (T == f64) h.record(value),\n            .drop => {}, // Intentionally do nothing\n        }\n        _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1c (lock-free)\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up aggregation resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .drop => {}, // Drop aggregation has no cleanup\n            else => {}, // Other aggregations don't need cleanup\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregation entries\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]AttributeAggregationEntry,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*AttributeAggregationEntry,\n\n    /// Initialize the attribute aggregation map\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry).init(allocator),\n            .aggregation_pool = undefined, // Will be initialized as needed\n            .next_free = 0,\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .cardinality = 0,\n            .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up aggregation entries that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit();\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n    ) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        _ = instrument; // Instrument pointer not used in Phase 1b, metadata hash sufficient\n        // Combine instrument metadata hash and attribute hash for unique key\n        const attr_hash = sdk.computeAttributeHash(attributes);\n        const combined_hash = (@as(u128, metadata.metadata_hash) << 64) | attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation entry already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |entry| {\n            return entry;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n        if (self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY) {\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        }\n\n        // Get next aggregation entry from pool\n        const entry = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation entry based on instrument type and value type\n        const owned_attributes = self.cloneAttributes(attributes) catch &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n        entry.* = .{\n            .aggregation = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                // On error, mark this slot as unused and return overflow\n                self.next_free -= 1;\n                self.cardinality -= 1;\n                return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n            },\n            .attributes = owned_attributes,\n            .allocator = self.allocator,\n        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, entry) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            entry.deinit();\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        return entry;\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *AttributeAggregationEntry = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation entry\n            overflow_slot.* = .{\n                .aggregation = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                    // If we can't create aggregation, create a minimal sum aggregation\n                    return self.createMinimalOverflowAggregation();\n                },\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // Empty attributes for overflow\n                .allocator = self.allocator,\n            };\n\n            self.overflow_aggregation = overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation entry\n        const entry = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        entry.* = .{\n            .aggregation = .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .instrument_unit = \"\",\n                },\n            },\n            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // Empty attributes for overflow\n            .allocator = self.allocator,\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = entry;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return entry;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (value) {\n                .i64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_i64 = hist };\n                },\n                .f64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_f64 = hist };\n                },\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clone attributes for owned storage\n    fn cloneAttributes(self: *@This(), attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) ![]api.AttributeKeyValue {\n        if (attributes.len == 0) return &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n        const owned_attrs = try self.allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, attributes.len);\n        for (attributes, 0..) |attr, i| {\n            const owned_key = try self.allocator.dupe(u8, attr.key);\n            owned_attrs[i] = .{\n                .key = owned_key,\n                .value = switch (attr.value) {\n                    .string => |s| .{ .string = try self.allocator.dupe(u8, s) },\n                    else => attr.value, // For non-string values, just copy the union\n                },\n            };\n        }\n        return owned_attrs;\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregation entries\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n    };\n\n    const attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    };\n\n    const instrument_ptr = @as(*anyopaque, @ptrFromInt(@as(usize, 0x1000)));\n\n    // Get aggregation for first time\n    const agg1 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(agg1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n    const agg2 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expectEqual(agg1, agg2); // Should be same pointer\n\n    // Different attributes should create new aggregation\n    const attributes2 = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"POST\" } },\n    };\n    const agg3 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes2, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(agg3 != agg1);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 2), map.getCardinality());\n}\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - cardinality limit\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    // Fill up to the limit (using a smaller limit for testing)\n    // Note: In practice, MAX_CARDINALITY is 2000, but for testing we can't easily create that many\n    // This test verifies the logic works\n    try testing.expect(!map.hasExceededLimit());\n}\n",

Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregations\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]Aggregation,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*Aggregation,\n\n    /// Initialize the attribute aggregation map\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).init(allocator),\n            .aggregation_pool = undefined, // Will be initialized as needed\n            .next_free = 0,\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .cardinality = 0,\n            .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up histogram aggregations that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit(self.allocator);\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n    ) *Aggregation {\n        _ = instrument; // Instrument pointer not used in Phase 1b, metadata hash sufficient\n        // Combine instrument metadata hash and attribute hash for unique key\n        const attr_hash = sdk.computeAttributeHash(attributes);\n        const combined_hash = (@as(u128, metadata.metadata_hash) << 64) | attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |agg| {\n            return agg;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n        if (self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY) {\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        }\n\n        // Get next aggregation from pool\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation based on instrument type and value type\n        agg.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n            // On error, mark this slot as unused and return overflow\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, agg) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            agg.deinit(self.allocator);\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) *Aggregation {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *Aggregation = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation\n            overflow_slot.* = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                // If we can't create aggregation, create a minimal sum aggregation\n                return self.createMinimalOverflowAggregation();\n            };\n\n            self.overflow_aggregation = overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *Aggregation {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation\n        const agg = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        agg.* = .{\n            .sum_i64 = .{\n                .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                .instrument_unit = \"\",\n            },\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = agg;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return agg;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (value) {\n                .i64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_i64 = hist };\n                },\n                .f64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_f64 = hist };\n                },\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregations\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *Aggregation).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n    };\n\n    const attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    };\n\n    const instrument_ptr = @as(*anyopaque, @ptrFromInt(@as(usize, 0x1000)));\n\n    // Get aggregation for first time\n    const agg1 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(agg1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n    const agg2 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expectEqual(agg1, agg2); // Should be same pointer\n\n    // Different attributes should create new aggregation\n    const attributes2 = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"POST\" } },\n    };\n    const agg3 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes2, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(agg3 != agg1);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 2), map.getCardinality());\n}\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - cardinality limit\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    // Fill up to the limit (using a smaller limit for testing)\n    // Note: In practice, MAX_CARDINALITY is 2000, but for testing we can't easily create that many\n    // This test verifies the logic works\n    try testing.expect(!map.hasExceededLimit());\n}\n",

This module manages aggregation state per reader, allowing multiple readers\n//! to maintain independent aggregation states for the same instruments.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const AttributeAggregationMap = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").AttributeAggregationMap;\n    const Aggregation = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").Aggregation;\n    const AttributeAggregationEntry = @import(\"attribute_aggregation_map.zig\").AttributeAggregationEntry;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n};\n\n/// Aggregation temporality for metric data points\npub const AggregationTemporality = enum {\n    Delta,\n    Cumulative,\n};\n\n/// Function type for selecting aggregation based on instrument type\npub const AggregationSelector = *const fn (instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType;\n\n/// Types of aggregations available\npub const AggregationType = enum {\n    sum,\n    last_value,\n    histogram,\n    drop, // Special case: don't aggregate at all\n};\n\n/// Default aggregation selector based on instrument type\npub fn defaultAggregationSelector(instrument_type: sdk.InstrumentType) AggregationType {\n    return switch (instrument_type) {\n        .Counter, .UpDownCounter, .ObservableCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .sum,\n        .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .last_value,\n        .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => .histogram,\n    };\n}\n\n/// Per-reader aggregation state management\npub const ReaderAggregationState = struct {\n    // Phase 1b: Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\n    aggregations: sdk.AttributeAggregationMap,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex, // Protects map access only in Phase 1c (aggregations are lock-free)\n\n    // Reader's configured temporality\n    temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n\n    // Aggregation selector (determines aggregation type per instrument)\n    aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n\n    // For cumulative temporality, track last collection time\n    last_collection_time_ns: u64,\n\n    /// Initialize reader aggregation state\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        temporality: AggregationTemporality,\n        aggregation_selector: AggregationSelector,\n    ) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = sdk.AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator),\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .temporality = temporality,\n            .aggregation_selector = aggregation_selector,\n            .last_collection_time_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all aggregations and resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Clean up the attribute aggregation map\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a measurement from an instrument (lock-free for aggregation updates)\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        // Phase 1c: Lock only for map access, aggregation updates are lock-free\n        const agg = blk: {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            // Get or create aggregation for this instrument + attribute combination\n            break :blk self.aggregations.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument, attributes, metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        // Record the measurement lock-free (aggregations use atomic operations)\n        switch (value) {\n            .i64 => |v| agg.aggregation.record(v, self.allocator),\n            .f64 => |v| agg.aggregation.record(v, self.allocator),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all aggregations (lock-free aggregation access)\n    pub fn collect(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]sdk.MetricData {\n        // Lock only for map iteration, aggregation data access is lock-free\n        var entry_list = std.ArrayList(*sdk.AttributeAggregationEntry).init(allocator);\n        defer entry_list.deinit();\n\n        // Copy aggregation entry pointers under lock\n        {\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n            var iter = self.aggregations.iterator();\n            while (iter.next()) |entry| {\n                try entry_list.append(entry.value_ptr.*);\n            }\n        }\n\n        var metrics_list = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(allocator);\n        errdefer metrics_list.deinit();\n\n        const current_timestamp = @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()));\n\n        // Process aggregation entries lock-free (atomic reads)\n        for (entry_list.items) |entry| {\n            // Create metric data based on aggregation type (lock-free atomic reads)\n            const metric_data = try self.createMetricDataFromAggregationEntry(\n                allocator,\n                entry,\n                current_timestamp,\n            );\n\n            if (metric_data) |data| {\n                try metrics_list.append(data);\n            }\n        }\n\n        return metrics_list.toOwnedSlice();\n    }\n\n    /// Convert a single aggregation entry to MetricData\n    fn createMetricDataFromAggregationEntry(\n        self: *@This(),\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        entry: *sdk.AttributeAggregationEntry,\n        timestamp: u64,\n    ) !?sdk.MetricData {\n        _ = self; // Not used in this helper method\n\n        // Create data points array with single point\n        const data_points = try allocator.alloc(sdk.MetricDataPoint, 1);\n        errdefer allocator.free(data_points);\n\n        // Clone attributes from the entry for export\n        const export_attributes = try allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, entry.attributes.len);\n        @memcpy(export_attributes, entry.attributes);\n\n        switch (entry.aggregation) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = export_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .i64_sum = sum.value.load(.monotonic) },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .sum_f64 => |*sum| {\n                data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                    .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = sum.start_timestamp_ns,\n                    .attributes = export_attributes,\n                    .value = .{ .f64_sum = sum.value.load(.monotonic) },\n                };\n                return sdk.MetricData{\n                    .name = sum.instrument_name,\n                    .description = null,\n                    .unit = if (sum.instrument_unit.len > 0) sum.instrument_unit else null,\n                    .type = .sum,\n                    .data_points = data_points,\n                    .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                        .name = \"unknown\",\n                        .version = null,\n                        .schema_url = null,\n                        .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                    },\n                    .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                };\n            },\n            .last_value_i64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.getValue()) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = export_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .i64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = gauge.instrument_name,\n                        .description = null,\n                        .unit = if (gauge.instrument_unit.len > 0) gauge.instrument_unit else null,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = api.InstrumentationScope{\n                            .name = \"unknown\",\n                            .version = null,\n                            .schema_url = null,\n                            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{},\n                        },\n                        .resource = sdk.Resource.empty,\n                    };\n                } else {\n                    // No value recorded, skip this gauge\n                    allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(export_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .last_value_f64 => |*gauge| {\n                if (gauge.getValue()) |value| {\n                    data_points[0] = sdk.MetricDataPoint{\n                        .timestamp_ns = timestamp,\n                        .start_timestamp_ns = null, // Gauges don't have start times\n                        .attributes = export_attributes,\n                        .value = .{ .f64_gauge = value },\n                    };\n                    return sdk.MetricData{\n allocator.free(data_points);\n                    allocator.free(export_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n            },\n            .histogram_i64 => |*hist| {\n allocator.free(export_attributes);\n                    return null;\n                }\n\n                // Copy atomic bucket counts to regular u64 array\n                const bucket_counts = try   "new_text": "//! Attribute Aggregation Map for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregations indexed by attribute combinations with\n//! cardinality limits and overflow handling as per OpenTelemetry specification.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const computeAttributeHash = @import(\"attribute_hash.zig\").computeAttributeHash;\n};\n\n/// Entry that combines aggregation with its attributes for export\npub const AttributeAggregationEntry = struct {\n    aggregation: Aggregation,\n    attributes: []api.AttributeKeyValue,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator, // For cleaning up owned attributes\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.aggregation.deinit(self.allocator);\n        // Free owned attributes\n        for (self.attributes) |attr| {\n            if (attr.value == .string) {\n                self.allocator.free(attr.key);\n                self.allocator.free(attr.value.string);\n            } else {\n                self.allocator.free(attr.key);\n            }\n        }\n        if (self.attributes.len > 0) {\n            self.allocator.free(self.attributes);\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Aggregation union type that supports all aggregation variants\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(i64),\n    sum_f64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(f64),\n    last_value_i64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(i64),\n    last_value_f64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(f64),\n    histogram_i64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64),\n    histogram_f64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64),\n    drop: void, // Drop aggregation - ignores all measurements\n\n    /// Record a measurement on this aggregation (lock-free)\n    pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: anytype, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        const T = @TypeOf(value);\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*s| if (T == i64) s.add(value),\n            .sum_f64 => |*s| if (T == f64) s.add(value),\n            .last_value_i64 => |*lv| if (T == i64) lv.record(value),\n            .last_value_f64 => |*lv| if (T == f64) lv.record(value),\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| if (T == i64) h.record(value),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| if (T == f64) h.record(value),\n            .drop => {}, // Intentionally do nothing\n        }\n        _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1c (lock-free)\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up aggregation resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .drop => {}, // Drop aggregation has no cleanup\n            else => {}, // Other aggregations don't need cleanup\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregation entries\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]AttributeAggregationEntry,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*AttributeAggregationEntry,\n\n    /// Initialize the attribute aggregation map\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry).init(allocator),\n            .aggregation_pool = undefined, // Will be initialized as needed\n            .next_free = 0,\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .cardinality = 0,\n            .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up aggregation entries that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit();\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n    ) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        _ = instrument; // Instrument pointer not used in Phase 1b, metadata hash sufficient\n        // Combine instrument metadata hash and attribute hash for unique key\n        const attr_hash = sdk.computeAttributeHash(attributes);\n        const combined_hash = (@as(u128, metadata.metadata_hash) << 64) | attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation entry already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |entry| {\n            return entry;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n        if (self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY) {\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        }\n\n        // Get next aggregation entry from pool\n        const entry = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation entry based on instrument type and value type\n        const owned_attributes = self.cloneAttributes(attributes) catch &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n        entry.* = .{\n            .aggregation = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                // On error, mark this slot as unused and return overflow\n                self.next_free -= 1;\n                self.cardinality -= 1;\n                return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n            },\n            .attributes = owned_attributes,\n            .allocator = self.allocator,\n        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, entry) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            entry.deinit();\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        return entry;\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *AttributeAggregationEntry = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation entry\n            overflow_slot.* = .{\n                .aggregation = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                    // If we can't create aggregation, create a minimal sum aggregation\n                    return self.createMinimalOverflowAggregation();\n                },\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // Empty attributes for overflow\n                .allocator = self.allocator,\n            };\n\n            self.overflow_aggregation = overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation entry\n        const entry = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        entry.* = .{\n            .aggregation = .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .instrument_unit = \"\",\n                },\n            },\n            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // Empty attributes for overflow\n            .allocator = self.allocator,\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = entry;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return entry;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (value) {\n                .i64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_i64 = hist };\n                },\n                .f64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_f64 = hist };\n                },\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clone attributes for owned storage\n    fn cloneAttributes(self: *@This(), attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) ![]api.AttributeKeyValue {\n        if (attributes.len == 0) return &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n        const owned_attrs = try self.allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, attributes.len);\n        for (attributes, 0..) |attr, i| {\n            const owned_key = try self.allocator.dupe(u8, attr.key);\n            owned_attrs[i] = .{\n                .key = owned_key,\n                .value = switch (attr.value) {\n                    .string => |s| .{ .string = try self.allocator.dupe(u8, s) },\n                    else => attr.value, // For non-string values, just copy the union\n                },\n            };\n        }\n        return owned_attrs;\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregation entries\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n    };\n\n    const attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    };\n\n    const instrument_ptr = @as(*anyopaque, @ptrFromInt(@as(usize, 0x1000)));\n\n    // Get aggregation for first time\n    const entry1 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(entry1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n    const entry2 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expectEqual(entry1, entry2); // Should be same pointer\n\n    // Different attributes should create new aggregation\n    const attributes2 = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"POST\" } },\n    };\n    const entry3 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes2, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(entry3 != entry1);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 2), map.getCardinality());\n}\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - cardinality limit\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    // Fill up to the limit (using a smaller limit for testing)\n    // Note: In practice, MAX_CARDINALITY is 2000, but for testing we can't easily create that many\n    // This test verifies the logic works\n    try testing.expect(!map.hasExceededLimit());\n}\n",
  "old_text": "//! Attribute Aggregation Map for OpenTelemetry Metrics SDK\n//!\n//! This module manages aggregations indexed by attribute combinations with\n//! cardinality limits and overflow handling as per OpenTelemetry specification.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const InstrumentType = @import(\"metadata.zig\").InstrumentType;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n    const computeAttributeHash = @import(\"attribute_hash.zig\").computeAttributeHash;\n};\n\n/// Entry that combines aggregation with its attributes for export\npub const AttributeAggregationEntry = struct {\n    aggregation: Aggregation,\n    attributes: []api.AttributeKeyValue,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator, // For cleaning up owned attributes\n\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        self.aggregation.deinit(self.allocator);\n        // Free owned attributes\n        for (self.attributes) |attr| {\n            if (attr.value == .string) {\n                self.allocator.free(attr.key);\n                self.allocator.free(attr.value.string);\n            } else {\n                self.allocator.free(attr.key);\n            }\n        }\n        if (self.attributes.len > 0) {\n            self.allocator.free(self.attributes);\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Aggregation union type that supports all aggregation variants\npub const Aggregation = union(enum) {\n    sum_i64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(i64),\n    sum_f64: sdk.aggregations.SumAggregation(f64),\n    last_value_i64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(i64),\n    last_value_f64: sdk.aggregations.LastValueAggregation(f64),\n    histogram_i64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64),\n    histogram_f64: sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64),\n    drop: void, // Drop aggregation - ignores all measurements\n\n    /// Record a measurement on this aggregation (lock-free)\n    pub fn record(self: *@This(), value: anytype, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        const T = @TypeOf(value);\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .sum_i64 => |*s| if (T == i64) s.add(value),\n            .sum_f64 => |*s| if (T == f64) s.add(value),\n            .last_value_i64 => |*lv| if (T == i64) lv.record(value),\n            .last_value_f64 => |*lv| if (T == f64) lv.record(value),\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| if (T == i64) h.record(value),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| if (T == f64) h.record(value),\n            .drop => {}, // Intentionally do nothing\n        }\n        _ = allocator; // Unused in Phase 1c (lock-free)\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up aggregation resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This(), allocator: std.mem.Allocator) void {\n        switch (self.*) {\n            .histogram_i64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .histogram_f64 => |*h| h.deinit(allocator),\n            .drop => {}, // Drop aggregation has no cleanup\n            else => {}, // Other aggregations don't need cleanup\n        }\n    }\n};\n\n/// Attribute-based aggregation map with cardinality limits\npub const AttributeAggregationMap = struct {\n    // Cardinality limit (matches OpenTelemetry spec default)\n    pub const MAX_CARDINALITY: usize = 2000;\n\n    // Map from (instrument_hash + attribute_hash) to aggregation\n    aggregations: std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry),\n\n    // Pre-allocated pool of aggregation entries\n    aggregation_pool: [MAX_CARDINALITY]AttributeAggregationEntry,\n    next_free: usize,\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n\n    // Track cardinality\n    cardinality: usize,\n\n    // Overflow aggregation for when limit is reached\n    overflow_aggregation: ?*AttributeAggregationEntry,\n\n    /// Initialize the attribute aggregation map\n    pub fn init(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) @This() {\n        return .{\n            .aggregations = std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry).init(allocator),\n            .aggregation_pool = undefined, // Will be initialized as needed\n            .next_free = 0,\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .cardinality = 0,\n            .overflow_aggregation = null,\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up all resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *@This()) void {\n        // Clean up aggregation entries that need allocator cleanup\n        for (0..self.next_free) |i| {\n            self.aggregation_pool[i].deinit();\n        }\n\n        self.aggregations.deinit();\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create aggregation for the given instrument and attribute combination\n    pub fn getOrCreateAggregation(\n        self: *@This(),\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n    ) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        _ = instrument; // Instrument pointer not used in Phase 1b, metadata hash sufficient\n        // Combine instrument metadata hash and attribute hash for unique key\n        const attr_hash = sdk.computeAttributeHash(attributes);\n        const combined_hash = (@as(u128, metadata.metadata_hash) << 64) | attr_hash;\n\n        // Check if aggregation entry already exists\n        if (self.aggregations.get(combined_hash)) |entry| {\n            return entry;\n        }\n\n        // Check cardinality limit\n        if (self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY) {\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        }\n\n        // Get next aggregation entry from pool\n        const entry = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free];\n        self.next_free += 1;\n        self.cardinality += 1;\n\n        // Initialize aggregation entry based on instrument type and value type\n        const owned_attributes = self.cloneAttributes(attributes) catch &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n        entry.* = .{\n            .aggregation = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                // On error, mark this slot as unused and return overflow\n                self.next_free -= 1;\n                self.cardinality -= 1;\n                return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n            },\n            .attributes = owned_attributes,\n            .allocator = self.allocator,\n        };\n\n        // Store in map\n        self.aggregations.put(combined_hash, entry) catch {\n            // On error, clean up and return overflow\n            entry.deinit();\n            self.next_free -= 1;\n            self.cardinality -= 1;\n            return self.getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(metadata, value);\n        };\n\n        return entry;\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create the overflow aggregation\n    fn getOrCreateOverflowAggregation(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *AttributeAggregationEntry = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation entry\n            overflow_slot.* = .{\n                .aggregation = self.createAggregationForType(metadata, value) catch {\n                    // If we can't create aggregation, create a minimal sum aggregation\n                    return self.createMinimalOverflowAggregation();\n                },\n                .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // Empty attributes for overflow\n                .allocator = self.allocator,\n            };\n\n            self.overflow_aggregation = overflow_slot;\n\n            // Store overflow aggregation in map\n            self.aggregations.put(overflow_key, overflow_slot) catch {\n                // If we can't store in map, still use it as overflow\n            };\n\n            // Report cardinality limit exceeded\n            api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation entry\n        const entry = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        entry.* = .{\n            .aggregation = .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .instrument_unit = \"\",\n                },\n            },\n            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // Empty attributes for overflow\n            .allocator = self.allocator,\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = entry;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return entry;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (value) {\n                .i64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_i64 = hist };\n                },\n                .f64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_f64 = hist };\n                },\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clone attributes for owned storage\n    fn cloneAttributes(self: *@This(), attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) ![]api.AttributeKeyValue {\n        if (attributes.len == 0) return &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n        const owned_attrs = try self.allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, attributes.len);\n        for (attributes, 0..) |attr, i| {\n            const owned_key = try self.allocator.dupe(u8, attr.key);\n            owned_attrs[i] = .{\n                .key = owned_key,\n                .value = switch (attr.value) {\n                    .string => |s| .{ .string = try self.allocator.dupe(u8, s) },\n                    else => attr.value, // For non-string values, just copy the union\n                },\n            };\n        }\n        return owned_attrs;\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregation entries\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n    };\n\n    const attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    };\n\n    const instrument_ptr = @as(*anyopaque, @ptrFromInt(@as(usize, 0x1000)));\n\n    // Get aggregation for first time\n    const agg1 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(agg1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n    const agg2 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expectEqual(agg1, agg2); // Should be same pointer\n\n    // Different attributes should create new aggregation\n    const attributes2 = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"POST\" } },\n    };\n    const agg3 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes2, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(agg3 != agg1);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 2), map.getCardinality());\n}\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - cardinality limit\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    // Fill up to the limit (using a smaller limit for testing)\n    // Note: In practice, MAX_CARDINALITY is 2000, but for testing we can't easily create that many\n    // This test verifies the logic works\n    try testing.expect(!map.hasExceededLimit());\n}\n",

if (self.overflow_aggregation == null) {\n            // Create overflow aggregation using a special overflow key\n            const overflow_key: u128 = std.math.maxInt(u128); // Special sentinel value\n\n            // Use first slot for overflow if not already used, otherwise find a slot\n            var overflow_slot: *AttributeAggregationEntry = undefined;\n            if (self.next_free == 0) {\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n                self.next_free = 1; // Reserve this slot\n            } else {\n                // Use last allocated slot for overflow\n                overflow_slot = &self.aggregation_pool[self.next_free - 1];\n            }\n\n            // Initialize overflow aggregation entry\n            overflow_slot.* = .{\n api.common.reportResourceExhaustedError(.meter, \"getOrCreateAggregation\", \"Attribute cardinality limit exceeded\", null);\n        }\n\n        return self.overflow_aggregation.?;\n    }\n\n    /// Create a minimal overflow aggregation when all else fails\n    fn createMinimalOverflowAggregation(self: *@This()) *AttributeAggregationEntry {\n        // Use first slot and create a minimal sum aggregation entry\n        const entry = &self.aggregation_pool[0];\n        entry.* = .{\n            .aggregation = .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = \"overflow\",\n                    .instrument_type = .Counter,\n                    .instrument_unit = \"\",\n                },\n            },\n            .attributes = &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{}, // Empty attributes for overflow\n            .allocator = self.allocator,\n        };\n        self.overflow_aggregation = entry;\n        self.next_free = @max(self.next_free, 1);\n        return entry;\n    }\n\n    /// Create aggregation of the appropriate type for the instrument\n    fn createAggregationForType(self: *@This(), metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata, value: sdk.MetricValue) !Aggregation {\n        return switch (metadata.instrument_type) {\n            .Counter, .ObservableCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .UpDownCounter, .ObservableUpDownCounter => .{\n                .sum_i64 = .{\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n                    .start_timestamp_ns = @intCast(std.time.nanoTimestamp()),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Gauge, .ObservableGauge => .{\n                .last_value_f64 = .{\n                    .has_value = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n                    .value = std.atomic.Value(f64).init(0),\n                    .instrument_name = metadata.name,\n                    .instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type,\n                    .instrument_unit = metadata.unit,\n                },\n            },\n            .Histogram, .ObservableHistogram => switch (value) {\n                .i64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(i64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_i64 = hist };\n                },\n                .f64 => blk: {\n                    var hist = try sdk.aggregations.HistogramAggregation(f64).init(\n                        self.allocator,\n                        .{ .boundaries = &sdk.aggregations.DEFAULT_HISTOGRAM_BOUNDARIES },\n                    );\n                    hist.instrument_name = metadata.name;\n                    hist.instrument_type = metadata.instrument_type;\n                    hist.instrument_unit = metadata.unit;\n                    break :blk .{ .histogram_f64 = hist };\n                },\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Clone attributes for owned storage\n    fn cloneAttributes(self: *@This(), attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue) ![]api.AttributeKeyValue {\n        if (attributes.len == 0) return &[_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n        const owned_attrs = try self.allocator.alloc(api.AttributeKeyValue, attributes.len);\n        for (attributes, 0..) |attr, i| {\n            const owned_key = try self.allocator.dupe(u8, attr.key);\n            owned_attrs[i] = .{\n                .key = owned_key,\n                .value = switch (attr.value) {\n                    .string => |s| .{ .string = try self.allocator.dupe(u8, s) },\n                    else => attr.value, // For non-string values, just copy the union\n                },\n            };\n        }\n        return owned_attrs;\n    }\n\n    /// Get current cardinality (number of unique attribute combinations)\n    pub fn getCardinality(self: *const @This()) usize {\n        return self.cardinality;\n    }\n\n    /// Check if cardinality limit has been exceeded\n    pub fn hasExceededLimit(self: *const @This()) bool {\n        return self.next_free >= MAX_CARDINALITY;\n    }\n\n    /// Iterator for all aggregation entries\n    pub fn iterator(self: *@This()) std.AutoHashMap(u128, *AttributeAggregationEntry).Iterator {\n        return self.aggregations.iterator();\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - basic operations\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    const metadata = sdk.MetricMetadata{\n        .name = \"test_counter\",\n        .description = \"Test counter\",\n        .unit = \"1\",\n        .instrument_type = .Counter,\n        .meter_name = \"test_meter\",\n        .meter_version = \"1.0.0\",\n        .meter_schema_url = \"\",\n        .metadata_hash = 12345,\n    };\n\n    const attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{\n        .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    };\n\n    const instrument_ptr = @as(*anyopaque, @ptrFromInt(@as(usize, 0x1000)));\n\n    // Get aggregation for first time\n    const entry1 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(@intFromPtr(entry1) != 0);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), map.getCardinality());\n\n    // Get same aggregation again\n    const entry2 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"POST\" } },\n    };\n    const entry3 = map.getOrCreateAggregation(instrument_ptr, &attributes2, metadata, .{ .i64 = 42 });\n    try testing.expect(entry3 != entry1);\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 2), map.getCardinality());\n}\n\ntest \"AttributeAggregationMap - cardinality limit\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    var map = AttributeAggregationMap.init(allocator);\n    defer map.deinit();\n\n    // Fill up to the limit (using a smaller limit for testing)\n    // Note: In practice,
