  "old_text": "//! Basic Periodic Metrics Processor\n//!\n//! This module provides a basic metrics processor that collects metrics from registered\n//! meters on a periodic basis using a background thread. It uses POSIX threads\n//! for cross-platform compatibility.\n//!\n//! The processor maintains a thread that wakes up at regular intervals to collect\n//! metrics from all registered meters and export them via the configured exporter.\n\nconst std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\nconst c = std.c;\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const BridgeReader = @import(\"reader.zig\").BridgeReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const Reader = @import(\"reader.zig\").Reader;\n    const MetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MetricExporter;\n    const ReaderAggregationState = @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").ReaderAggregationState;\n    const MetricMetadata = @import(\"metadata.zig\").MetricMetadata;\n    const MetricValue = @import(\"reader.zig\").MetricValue;\n};\n\n/// Basic periodic metrics processor that collects metrics at regular intervals\npub const PeriodicReader = struct {\n    pub const PipelineStep = @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineStepInstructions(\n        PeriodicReader,\n        sdk.Reader,\n        ?u32,\n        reader,\n        _initFn,\n        setExporter,\n    );\n    pub fn _initFn(self: *PeriodicReader, interval: ?u32, allocator: std.mem.Allocator) !void {\n        self.* = init(allocator, null, interval);\n        try self.start();\n    }\n\n    allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n    exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n    mutex: std.Thread.Mutex,\n    condition: std.Thread.Condition,\n    is_shutdown: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    is_running: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    collection_in_progress: std.atomic.Value(bool),\n    flush_complete: std.Thread.Condition,\n    collection_complete: std.Thread.Condition,\n    last_collection_time: std.atomic.Value(i64),\n    thread: ?std.Thread,\n    collection_interval_ms: u32,\n    registered_meters: std.ArrayListUnmanaged(*sdk.Meter),\n    reader_state: sdk.ReaderAggregationState,\n\n    /// Initialize a new basic periodic metrics processor\n    /// collection_interval_ms: How often to collect metrics (default: 60000ms = 60s)\n    pub fn init(\n        allocator: std.mem.Allocator,\n        exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter,\n        collection_interval_ms: ?u32,\n    ) PeriodicReader {\n        return .{\n            .allocator = allocator,\n            .exporter = exporter,\n            .mutex = .{},\n            .condition = .{},\n            .is_shutdown = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .is_running = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .collection_in_progress = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(false),\n            .flush_complete = .{},\n            .collection_complete = .{},\n            .last_collection_time = std.atomic.Value(i64).init(0),\n            .thread = null,\n            .collection_interval_ms = collection_interval_ms orelse 60000, // 60 seconds default\n            .registered_meters = .{},\n            .reader_state = sdk.ReaderAggregationState.init(\n                allocator,\n                .Delta, // Default to Delta temporality for now\n                @import(\"reader_aggregation_state.zig\").defaultAggregationSelector,\n            ),\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Start the background collection thread\n    pub fn start(self: *PeriodicReader) !void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_running.load(.acquire) or self.thread != null) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(true, .release);\n        self.thread = try std.Thread.spawn(.{}, collectionThreadFn, .{self});\n    }\n\n    /// Stop the background collection thread and clean up resources\n    pub fn deinit(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        // Signal shutdown\n        self.is_shutdown.store(true, .release);\n        self.is_running.store(false, .release);\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        self.condition.signal();\n        self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Wait for thread to finish\n        if (self.thread) |thread| {\n            thread.join();\n        }\n\n        // Clean up resources\n        self.reader_state.deinit();\n        self.registered_meters.deinit(self.allocator);\n        if (self.exporter) |exporter| {\n            exporter.deinit();\n            exporter.destroy();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Destroy the processor and free its memory\n    pub fn destroy(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        self.allocator.destroy(self);\n    }\n\n    pub fn recordMeasurement(\n        self: *PeriodicReader,\n        instrument: *anyopaque,\n        value: sdk.MetricValue,\n        attributes: []const api.AttributeKeyValue,\n        metadata: sdk.MetricMetadata,\n    ) void {\n        self.reader_state.recordMeasurement(instrument, value, attributes, metadata);\n    }\n\n    /// Collect metrics from all registered meters (called by background thread)\n    pub fn collect(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n        const arena_allocator = arena.allocator();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        // Collect from reader state instead of iterating meters\n        const collected_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena_allocator) catch {\n            // Log error if needed\n            return;\n        };\n\n        // Export all collected metrics. Exporter must copy memory\n        // that it needs beyond the duration of this call.\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| _ = exporter.exportMetrics(collected_metrics);\n        // Arena cleans up all the memory.\n    }\n\n    /// Force flush the exporter\n    pub fn forceFlush(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        // Quick check without mutex\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n            return .failure;\n        }\n\n        const start_time = std.time.milliTimestamp();\n\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        // Try to set flush_in_progress atomically\n        const was_flushing = self.flush_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst);\n        if (was_flushing) {\n            // Another flush is in progress, wait for it\n            const remaining_ms = if (timeout_ms) |ms|\n                ms -| @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.milliTimestamp() - start_time))\n            else\n                null;\n\n            if (remaining_ms == 0) {\n                return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.flush_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.flush_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        defer {\n            self.flush_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.flush_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Wait for any collection in progress\n        while (self.collection_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n            const remaining_ms = if (timeout_ms) |ms|\n                ms -| @as(u64, @intCast(std.time.milliTimestamp() - start_time))\n            else\n                null;\n\n            if (remaining_ms == 0) {\n                return .timeout;\n            }\n\n            if (remaining_ms) |ms| {\n                self.collection_complete.timedWait(&self.mutex, ms * std.time.ns_per_ms) catch {\n                    return .timeout;\n                };\n            } else {\n                self.collection_complete.wait(&self.mutex);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Now do immediate collection with atomic flag\n        self.collection_in_progress.store(true, .release);\n        defer {\n            self.collection_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n            self.collection_complete.broadcast();\n        }\n\n        // Perform immediate metric collection\n        var arena = std.heap.ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator);\n        defer arena.deinit();\n\n        // Collect from reader state (regular instruments)\n        var collected_metrics = std.ArrayList(sdk.MetricData).init(arena.allocator());\n\n        const reader_state_metrics = self.reader_state.collect(arena.allocator()) catch {\n            return .failure;\n        };\n        collected_metrics.appendSlice(reader_state_metrics) catch return .failure;\n\n        // Collect from observable instruments in registered meters\n        for (self.registered_meters.items) |meter| {\n            // Collect from i64 observable counters\n            for (meter.observable_counters_i64.items) |obs_counter| {\n                const data_points = obs_counter.collect(arena.allocator()) catch continue;\n                if (data_points.len > 0) {\n                    const metric_data = sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = obs_counter.name,\n                        .description = obs_counter.description,\n                        .unit = obs_counter.unit,\n                        .type = .sum,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = meter.scope,\n                        .resource = meter.resource,\n                    };\n                    collected_metrics.append(metric_data) catch continue;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Collect from f64 observable counters\n            for (meter.observable_counters_f64.items) |obs_counter| {\n                const data_points = obs_counter.collect(arena.allocator()) catch continue;\n                if (data_points.len > 0) {\n                    const metric_data = sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = obs_counter.name,\n                        .description = obs_counter.description,\n                        .unit = obs_counter.unit,\n                        .type = .sum,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = meter.scope,\n                        .resource = meter.resource,\n                    };\n                    collected_metrics.append(metric_data) catch continue;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Collect from i64 observable gauges\n            for (meter.observable_gauges_i64.items) |obs_gauge| {\n                const data_points = obs_gauge.collect(arena.allocator()) catch continue;\n                if (data_points.len > 0) {\n                    const metric_data = sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = obs_gauge.name,\n                        .description = obs_gauge.description,\n                        .unit = obs_gauge.unit,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = meter.scope,\n                        .resource = meter.resource,\n                    };\n                    collected_metrics.append(metric_data) catch continue;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Collect from f64 observable gauges\n            for (meter.observable_gauges_f64.items) |obs_gauge| {\n                const data_points = obs_gauge.collect(arena.allocator()) catch continue;\n                if (data_points.len > 0) {\n                    const metric_data = sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = obs_gauge.name,\n                        .description = obs_gauge.description,\n                        .unit = obs_gauge.unit,\n                        .type = .gauge,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = meter.scope,\n                        .resource = meter.resource,\n                    };\n                    collected_metrics.append(metric_data) catch continue;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Collect from i64 observable up-down counters\n            for (meter.observable_updown_counters_i64.items) |obs_updown| {\n                const data_points = obs_updown.collect(arena.allocator()) catch continue;\n                if (data_points.len > 0) {\n                    const metric_data = sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = obs_updown.name,\n                        .description = obs_updown.description,\n                        .unit = obs_updown.unit,\n                        .type = .sum,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = meter.scope,\n                        .resource = meter.resource,\n                    };\n                    collected_metrics.append(metric_data) catch continue;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Collect from f64 observable up-down counters\n            for (meter.observable_updown_counters_f64.items) |obs_updown| {\n                const data_points = obs_updown.collect(arena.allocator()) catch continue;\n                if (data_points.len > 0) {\n                    const metric_data = sdk.MetricData{\n                        .name = obs_updown.name,\n                        .description = obs_updown.description,\n                        .unit = obs_updown.unit,\n                        .type = .sum,\n                        .data_points = data_points,\n                        .scope = meter.scope,\n                        .resource = meter.resource,\n                    };\n                    collected_metrics.append(metric_data) catch continue;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update last collection time atomically\n        self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n        // Export if we have metrics\n        if (collected_metrics.len > 0 and self.exporter != null) {\n            // Temporarily release mutex for export\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const export_result = self.exporter.?.exportMetrics(collected_metrics.items);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            if (export_result != .success) {\n                return .failure;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Flush the exporter\n        if (self.exporter) |*exporter| {\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            const flush_result = exporter.forceFlush(timeout_ms);\n            self.mutex.lock();\n\n            return if (flush_result == .success) .success else .failure;\n        }\n\n        return .success;\n    }\n\n    /// Shutdown the processor\n    pub fn shutdown(self: *PeriodicReader, timeout_ms: ?u64) api.common.ProcessResult {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.swap(true, .seq_cst)) {\n            return .success;\n        }\n\n        self.is_running.store(false, .release);\n        self.condition.signal();\n\n        // Shutdown the exporter\n        const result = if (self.exporter) |*exporter| exporter.shutdown(timeout_ms) else .success;\n        return if (result == .success) .success else .failure;\n    }\n\n    /// Register a meter for periodic collection\n    pub fn registerMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        self.registered_meters.append(self.allocator, meter) catch {\n            // Handle allocation failure silently for now\n            return;\n        };\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a meter from periodic collection\n    pub fn unregisterMeter(self: *PeriodicReader, meter: *sdk.Meter) void {\n        self.mutex.lock();\n        defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n        if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) return;\n\n        for (self.registered_meters.items, 0..) |registered_meter, i| {\n            if (registered_meter == meter) {\n                _ = self.registered_meters.swapRemove(i);\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the exporter for this processor\n    pub fn setExporter(self: *PeriodicReader, exporter: ?sdk.MetricExporter) !void {\n        if (self.exporter) |old_exporter| {\n            old_exporter.deinit();\n            old_exporter.destroy();\n        }\n        self.exporter = exporter;\n    }\n\n    /// Get the processor as a MetricProcessor union\n    pub fn reader(self: *PeriodicReader) sdk.Reader {\n        return .{ .bridge = sdk.BridgeReader.init(self) };\n    }\n\n    /// Background thread function that periodically collects metrics\n    fn collectionThreadFn(self: *PeriodicReader) void {\n        while (true) {\n            // Fast path check without mutex\n            if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            self.mutex.lock();\n            defer self.mutex.unlock();\n\n            // Double-check under mutex\n            if (self.is_shutdown.load(.acquire)) {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            // Calculate wait time in nanoseconds\n            const wait_ns = @as(u64, self.collection_interval_ms) * std.time.ns_per_ms;\n\n            // Wait for the specified interval or until signaled\n            self.condition.timedWait(&self.mutex, wait_ns) catch {\n                // Timeout - normal collection cycle\n            };\n\n            // Skip if flush is in progress\n            if (self.flush_in_progress.load(.acquire)) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Try to acquire collection lock\n            if (self.collection_in_progress.swap(true, .seq_cst)) {\n                // Already collecting, skip this cycle\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            defer {\n                self.collection_in_progress.store(false, .release);\n                self.collection_complete.broadcast();\n            }\n\n            // Update last collection time\n            self.last_collection_time.store(std.time.milliTimestamp(), .release);\n\n            // Do the collection\n            self.mutex.unlock();\n            self.collect();\n            self.mutex.lock();\n        }\n    }\n};\n\ntest \"BasicPeriodicProcessor - direct init vs pipeline init thread behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n    const MockExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MockMetricExporter;\n    const Resource = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").Resource;\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockExporter.init(allocator);\n\n    // Create processor with very short interval for testing (direct init)\n    var processor = PeriodicReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter(), 100); // 100ms\n    defer processor.deinit();\n\n    // Verify thread is not running when created via direct init()\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Create a basic meter for testing\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const resource = Resource.empty;\n    const basic_meter = try allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n    basic_meter.* = try sdk.Meter.init(allocator, scope, resource, null);\n    defer {\n        basic_meter.deinit();\n        allocator.destroy(basic_meter);\n    }\n\n    // Start the thread manually (since we're not using pipeline)\n    try processor.start();\n\n    // Verify thread is now running\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread != null);\n\n    // Register meter\n    processor.registerMeter(basic_meter);\n\n    // Create a second meter\n    const scope2 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter2\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const basic_meter2 = try allocator.create(sdk.Meter);\n    basic_meter2.* = try sdk.Meter.init(allocator, scope2, resource, null);\n    defer {\n        basic_meter2.deinit();\n        allocator.destroy(basic_meter2);\n    }\n\n    // Register second meter\n    processor.registerMeter(basic_meter2);\n\n    // Verify thread is still running and we have both meters\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread != null);\n    try testing.expect(processor.registered_meters.items.len == 2);\n\n    // Wait a bit to allow some collections to happen\n    std.time.sleep(250 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n\n    // Verify some exports happened (thread is working)\n    const export_count = mock_exporter.exportCount();\n    try testing.expect(export_count > 0);\n\n    // Test shutdown - should stop the thread\n    _ = processor.shutdown(1000);\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_shutdown.load(.acquire));\n\n    // Give thread time to exit\n    std.time.sleep(50 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicPeriodicProcessor - no thread start without meters\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    // Create processor\n    var processor = PeriodicReader.init(allocator, null, 100);\n    defer processor.deinit();\n\n    // Verify thread is not running\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Call forceFlush - should not crash\n    const result = processor.forceFlush(100);\n    try testing.expect(result == .success);\n\n    // Thread should still not be running\n    try testing.expect(!processor.is_running.load(.acquire));\n    try testing.expect(processor.thread == null);\n\n    // Shutdown should work fine\n    _ = processor.shutdown(100);\n    try testing.expect(processor.is_shutdown.load(.acquire));\n}\n",

     }
+

.finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n    // Processor takes ownership of this memory\n\n    // Create periodic reader with 100ms interval\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.PeriodicReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.PeriodicReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter(), 100);\n\n    // Register reader (provider takes ownership)\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Get meter\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create regular instruments\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const gauge = &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_gauge = try meter.createObservableGauge(f64, \"test.observable.gauge\", \"Test observable gauge\", \"ratio\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_updown = try meter.createObservableUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.observable.updown\", \"Test observable updown\", \"items\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n\n    // Record some data with regular instruments\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n histogram.record(ctx, 25, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Setup callbacks for observable instruments\n    const counter_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(42, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const gauge_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(f64)) void {\n            result.observe(0.95, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const updown_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(100, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n\n    // Register the callbacks\n updown_handle.unregister();\n    }\n\n    // Wait for periodic collection to happen (simulate time passing)\n    std.time.sleep(200 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n\n    // Force a final collection\n testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.gauge, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.updown\")) {\n                found_observable_updown = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: found_counter={}, found_gauge={}, found_histogram={}\\n\", .{ found_counter, found_gauge, found_histogram });\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: found_observable_counter={}, found_observable_gauge={}, found_observable_updown={}\\n\", .{ found_observable_counter, found_observable_gauge, found_observable_updown });\n\n    try testing.expect(found_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_gauge);\n    try testing.expect(found_histogram);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_gauge);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_updown);\n}\n",
  "old_text": "const std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const ManualReader = @import(\"manual_reader.zig\").ManualReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MeterProvider = @import(\"meter_provider.zig\").MeterProvider;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricType = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricType;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const PeriodicReader = @import(\"periodic_reader.zig\").PeriodicReader;\n    const ResourceBuilder = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").ResourceBuilder;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n};\n\n// Import test dependencies\nconst MockMetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MockMetricExporter;\n\n// Helper function to cleanup MetricData memory\nfn cleanupMetrics(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, metrics: []sdk.MetricData) void {\n    for (metrics) |metric| {\n        // Free histogram bucket_counts if present (must do this before freeing data_points)\n        for (metric.data_points) |data_point| {\n            switch (data_point.value) {\n                .i64_histogram => |hist| allocator.free(hist.bucket_counts),\n                .f64_histogram => |hist| allocator.free(hist.bucket_counts),\n                else => {},\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Free data_points array\n        allocator.free(metric.data_points);\n    }\n    allocator.free(metrics);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider lifecycle\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    // Create resource\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    // Create provider (takes ownership of resource)\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    try testing.expect(provider.readers.items.len == 0);\n    try testing.expect(provider.cache.count() == 0);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider meter caching\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope1 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const scope2 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\"); // Same\n    const scope3 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"other.meter\", \"1.0.0\"); // Different\n\n    const meter1 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope1);\n    const meter2 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope2);\n    const meter3 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope3);\n\n    // Same scope should return same meter instance\n    try testing.expect(meter1.bridge.meter_ptr == meter2.bridge.meter_ptr);\n    try testing.expect(meter1.bridge.meter_ptr != meter3.bridge.meter_ptr);\n\n    // Verify cache contains 2 unique entries\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(u32, 2), provider.cache.count());\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider processor registration\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    try @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineBuilder(*sdk.MeterProvider).init(&provider)\n        .with(sdk.ManualReader.PipelineStep.init({}).flowTo(MockMetricExporter.PipelineStep.init({})))\n        .done();\n\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), provider.readers.items.len);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter instrument creation and data collection\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create various instrument types\n    const counter_i64 = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.i64\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const counter_f64 = try meter.createCounter(f64, \"test.counter.f64\", \"Test counter\", \"seconds\", null);\n    const updown_i64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.updown.i64\", \"Test updown\", \"connections\", null);\n    const gauge_i64 = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.i64\", \"Test gauge\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const histogram_f64 = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.f64\", \"Test histogram\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    // Record some measurements\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_f64.add(ctx, 3.14, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, -2, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge_i64.record(ctx, 42, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 15.5, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1 - Collection now happens at reader level, fix in Phase 1b\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance for direct collectMetrics //     }\n    // }\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter data collection through processor pipeline\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n    // Processor takes ownership of this memory\n\n    // Create processor (heap-allocated)\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.ManualReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.ManualReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter());\n\n    // Register reader (provider takes ownership)\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Get meter\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create instruments and record data\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"http.requests\", \"HTTP requests\", \"requests\", null);\n    const histogram = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"http.duration\", \"HTTP duration\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    counter.add(ctx, 3, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 12.5, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Force collection via processor (pull model)\n    reader.collect();\n\n    // Verify metrics were exported\n    try testing.expect(mock_exporter.metricCount() > 0);\n\n    // Check that we have the expected metric types\n    var found_counter = false;\n    var found_histogram = false;\n\n    for (0..mock_exporter.metricCount()) |i| {\n        if (mock_exporter.getMetric(i)) |metric| {\n            if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"http.requests\")) {\n                found_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"http.duration\")) {\n                found_histogram = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.histogram, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    try testing.expect(found_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_histogram);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter shutdown behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Get const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.ManualReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.ManualReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter());\n\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Test successful flush\n    const result = provider.forceFlush(1000);\n    try testing.expectEqual(api.common.FlushResult.success, result);\n\n    // Test flush with failure\n    mock_exporter.flush_result = .failure;\n    const result2 = provider.forceFlush(1000);\n    try testing.expectEqual(api.common.FlushResult.failure, result2);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter comprehensive instrument test with attributes\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Test custom histogram boundaries (for future use)\n    _ = [_]f64{ 0.0, 1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 50.0 };\n\n    // Create all instrument types with different value types\n    const counter_i64 = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.i64\", \"Test i64 counter\", \"ops\", null);\n    const counter_f64 = try meter.createCounter(f64, \"test.counter.f64\", \"Test f64 counter\", \"seconds\", null);\n    const updown_i64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.updown.i64\", \"Test i64 updown\", \"items\", null);\n    const updown_f64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(f64, \"test.updown.f64\", \"Test f64 updown\", \"temperature\", null);\n    const gauge_i64 = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.i64\", \"Test i64 gauge\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const gauge_f64 = try meter.createGauge(f64, \"test.gauge.f64\", \"Test f64 gauge\", \"ratio\", 6.0\n\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, -3, &empty_attributes); // Total: 7\n    updown_f64.add(ctx, 5.5, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_f64.add(ctx, -1.5, &empty_attributes); // bucket 1 (1-5)\n    histogram_i64.record(ctx, 7, &empty_attributes); // bucket 2 (5-10)\n    histogram_i64.record(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes); // bucket 3 (10-50)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 0.5, &empty_attributes); // bucket 0 (0-1)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 3.2, &empty_attributes); // bucket 1 (1-5)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes); testing.expect(gauges_found >= 2); // 2 gauges\n    // try testing.expect(histograms_found >= 2); // 2 histograms\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter instrument creation after shutdown returns noop instruments\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create instrument before shutdown - should be normal SDK instrument\n    const counter_before = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.before\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    try testing.expect(counter_before == .bridge); // Should be bridge to SDK instrument\n\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance to shutdown directly\n    const basic_meter: *sdk.Meter = @ptrCast(@alignCast(meter.bridge.meter_ptr));\n    basic_meter.shutdown();\n\n    // Create instruments after shutdown - should be noop instruments\n    const counter_after = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.after\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const updown_after = try meter.createUpDownCounter(f64, \"test.updown.after\", \"Test updown\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const gauge_after = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.after\", \"Test gauge\", \"items\", null);\n    const histogram_after = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.after\", \"Test histogram\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    // All instruments created after shutdown should be noop\n    try testing.expect(counter_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(updown_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(gauge_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(histogram_after == .noop);\n\n    // Verify noop instruments have the correct names\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.counter.after\", counter_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.updown.after\", updown_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.gauge.after\", gauge_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.histogram.after\", histogram_after.noop);\n}\n\ntest \"PeriodicReader with multiple instruments\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n    // Processor takes ownership of this memory\n\n    // Create periodic reader with 100ms interval\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.PeriodicReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.PeriodicReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter(), 100);\n\n    // Register reader (provider takes ownership)\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Get meter\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create regular instruments\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const gauge = try meter.createGauge(f64, \"test.gauge\", \"Test gauge\", \"ratio\", null);\n    const histogram = try meter.createHistogram(i64, \"test.histogram\", \"Test histogram\", \"count\", null);\n\n    // Create observable instruments\n    const observable_counter = try meter.createObservableCounter(i64, \"test.observable.counter\", \"Test observable counter\", \"requests\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_gauge = try meter.createObservableGauge(f64, \"test.observable.gauge\", \"Test observable gauge\", \"ratio\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_updown = try meter.createObservableUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.observable.updown\", \"Test observable updown\", \"items\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n\n    // Record some data with regular instruments\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge.record(ctx, 0.75, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 25, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Setup callbacks for observable instruments\n    const counter_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(42, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const gauge_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(f64)) void {\n            result.observe(0.95, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const updown_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(100, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n\n    // Register the callbacks\n    const counter_handle = try observable_counter.registerCallbackNoState(counter_callback);\n    const gauge_handle = try observable_gauge.registerCallbackNoState(gauge_callback);\n    const updown_handle = try observable_updown.registerCallbackNoState(updown_callback);\n\n    defer {\n        counter_handle.unregister();\n        gauge_handle.unregister();\n        updown_handle.unregister();\n    }\n\n    // Wait for periodic collection to happen (simulate time passing)\n    std.time.sleep(200 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n\n    // Force a final collection\n    reader.collect();\n\n    // Verify metrics were exported\n    try testing.expect(mock_exporter.metricCount() > 0);\n\n    // Check that we have the expected metric types\n    var found_counter = false;\n    var found_gauge = false;\n    var found_histogram = false;\n    var found_observable_counter = false;\n    var found_observable_gauge = false;\n    var found_observable_updown = false;\n\n    for (0..mock_exporter.metricCount()) |i| {\n        if (mock_exporter.getMetric(i)) |metric| {\n            if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.counter\")) {\n                found_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.gauge\")) {\n                found_gauge = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.gauge, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.histogram\")) {\n                found_histogram = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.histogram, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.counter\")) {\n                found_observable_counter = true;\n basic_meter: *sdk.Meter = @ptrCast(@alignCast(meter.bridge.meter_ptr));\n    basic_meter.shutdown();\n\n    // Create instruments after shutdown - should be noop instruments\n    const counter_after = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.after\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const updown_after = try meter.createUpDownCounter(f64, \"test.updown.after\", \"Test updown\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const gauge_after = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.after\", \"Test gauge\", \"items\", null);\n    const histogram_after = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.after\", \"Test histogram\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    // All instruments created after shutdown should be noop\n    try testing.expect(counter_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(updown_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(gauge_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(histogram_after == .noop);\n\n    // Verify noop instruments have the correct names\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.counter.after\", counter_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.updown.after\", updown_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.gauge.after\", gauge_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.histogram.after\", histogram_after.noop);\n}\n\ntest \"PeriodicReader with multiple instruments\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n    // Processor takes ownership of this memory\n\n    // Create periodic reader with 100ms interval\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.PeriodicReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.PeriodicReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter(), 100);\n\n    // Register reader (provider takes ownership)\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Get meter\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create regular instruments\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const gauge = try meter.createGauge(f64, \"test.gauge\", \"Test gauge\", \"ratio\", null);\n    const histogram = try meter.createHistogram(i64, \"test.histogram\", \"Test histogram\", \"count\", null);\n\n    // Create observable instruments\n    const observable_counter = try meter.createObservableCounter(i64, \"test.observable.counter\", \"Test observable counter\", \"requests\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_gauge = try meter.createObservableGauge(f64, \"test.observable.gauge\", \"Test observable gauge\", \"ratio\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_updown = try meter.createObservableUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.observable.updown\", \"Test observable updown\", \"items\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: Created observable instruments: counter={}, gauge={}, updown={}\\n\", .{ @intFromPtr(observable_counter.ptr), @intFromPtr(observable_gauge.ptr), @intFromPtr(observable_updown.ptr) });\n\n    // Record some data with regular instruments\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge.record(ctx, 0.75, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 25, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Setup callbacks for observable instruments\n    const counter_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(42, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const gauge_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(f64)) void {\n            result.observe(0.95, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const updown_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(100, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n\n    // Register the callbacks\n    const counter_handle = try observable_counter.registerCallbackNoState(counter_callback);\n    const gauge_handle = try observable_gauge.registerCallbackNoState(gauge_callback);\n    const updown_handle = try observable_updown.registerCallbackNoState(updown_callback);\n\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: Registered callbacks: counter_handle={}, gauge_handle={}, updown_handle={}\\n\", .{ @intFromPtr(counter_handle.ptr), @intFromPtr(gauge_handle.ptr), @intFromPtr(updown_handle.ptr) });\n\n    defer {\n        counter_handle.unregister();\n        gauge_handle.unregister();\n        updown_handle.unregister();\n    }\n\n    // Wait for periodic collection to happen (simulate time passing)\n    std.time.sleep(200 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n\n    // Debug: Check registered meters with reader before collection\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: Reader has {} registered meters\\n\", .{reader.registered_meters.items.len});\n    for (reader.registered_meters.items, 0..) |registered_meter, i| {\n        std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: Meter {}: {} observable counters i64, {} observable gauges i64, {} observable updown i64\\n\", .{ i, registered_meter.observable_counters_i64.items.len, registered_meter.observable_gauges_i64.items.len, registered_meter.observable_updown_counters_i64.items.len });\n    }\n\n    // Force a final collection\n    reader.collect();\n\n    // Verify metrics were exported\n    try testing.expect(mock_exporter.metricCount() > 0);\n\n    // Check that we have the expected metric types\n    var found_counter = false;\n    var found_gauge = false;\n    var found_histogram = false;\n    var found_observable_counter = false;\n    var found_observable_gauge = false;\n    var found_observable_updown = false;\n\n    std.debug.print(\"\\nDEBUG: Total metrics exported: {}\\n\", .{mock_exporter.metricCount()});\n    for (0..mock_exporter.metricCount()) |i| {\n        if (mock_exporter.getMetric(i)) |metric| {\n            std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: Metric {}: name='{s}', type={}, data_points={}\\n\", .{ i, metric.name, metric.type, metric.data_points.len });\n            if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.counter\")) {\n                found_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.gauge\")) {\n                found_gauge = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.gauge, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.histogram\")) {\n                found_histogram = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.histogram, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.counter\")) {\n                found_observable_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.gauge\")) {\n                found_observable_gauge = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.gauge, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.updown\")) {\n                found_observable_updown = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: found_counter={}, found_gauge={}, found_histogram={}\\n\", .{ found_counter, found_gauge, found_histogram });\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: found_observable_counter={}, found_observable_gauge={}, found_observable_updown={}\\n\", .{ found_observable_counter, found_observable_gauge, found_observable_updown });\n\n    try testing.expect(found_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_gauge);\n    try testing.expect(found_histogram);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_gauge);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_updown);\n}\n",
  "old_text": "const std = @import(\"std\");\nconst api = @import(\"otel-api\");\n\nconst sdk = struct {\n    const ManualReader = @import(\"manual_reader.zig\").ManualReader;\n    const Meter = @import(\"meter.zig\").Meter;\n    const MeterProvider = @import(\"meter_provider.zig\").MeterProvider;\n    const MetricData = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricData;\n    const MetricType = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricType;\n    const MetricDataPoint = @import(\"data.zig\").MetricDataPoint;\n    const PeriodicReader = @import(\"periodic_reader.zig\").PeriodicReader;\n    const ResourceBuilder = @import(\"../resource/resource.zig\").ResourceBuilder;\n    const aggregations = @import(\"aggregations.zig\");\n};\n\n// Import test dependencies\nconst MockMetricExporter = @import(\"exporter.zig\").MockMetricExporter;\n\n// Helper function to cleanup MetricData memory\nfn cleanupMetrics(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, metrics: []sdk.MetricData) void {\n    for (metrics) |metric| {\n        // Free histogram bucket_counts if present (must do this before freeing data_points)\n        for (metric.data_points) |data_point| {\n            switch (data_point.value) {\n                .i64_histogram => |hist| allocator.free(hist.bucket_counts),\n                .f64_histogram => |hist| allocator.free(hist.bucket_counts),\n                else => {},\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Free data_points array\n        allocator.free(metric.data_points);\n    }\n    allocator.free(metrics);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider lifecycle\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    // Create resource\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    // Create provider (takes ownership of resource)\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    try testing.expect(provider.readers.items.len == 0);\n    try testing.expect(provider.cache.count() == 0);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider meter caching\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope1 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    const scope2 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\"); // Same\n    const scope3 = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"other.meter\", \"1.0.0\"); // Different\n\n    const meter1 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope1);\n    const meter2 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope2);\n    const meter3 = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope3);\n\n    // Same scope should return same meter instance\n    try testing.expect(meter1.bridge.meter_ptr == meter2.bridge.meter_ptr);\n    try testing.expect(meter1.bridge.meter_ptr != meter3.bridge.meter_ptr);\n\n    // Verify cache contains 2 unique entries\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(u32, 2), provider.cache.count());\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider processor registration\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    try @import(\"../common/pipeline.zig\").PipelineBuilder(*sdk.MeterProvider).init(&provider)\n        .with(sdk.ManualReader.PipelineStep.init({}).flowTo(MockMetricExporter.PipelineStep.init({})))\n        .done();\n\n    try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 1), provider.readers.items.len);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter instrument creation and data collection\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create various instrument types\n    const counter_i64 = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.i64\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const counter_f64 = try meter.createCounter(f64, \"test.counter.f64\", \"Test counter\", \"seconds\", null);\n    const updown_i64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.updown.i64\", \"Test updown\", \"connections\", null);\n    const gauge_i64 = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.i64\", \"Test gauge\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const histogram_f64 = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.f64\", \"Test histogram\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    // Record some measurements\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_f64.add(ctx, 3.14, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, -2, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge_i64.record(ctx, 42, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 15.5, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1 - Collection now happens at reader level, fix in Phase 1b\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance for direct collectMetrics call\n    // const basic_meter: *sdk.Meter = @ptrCast(@alignCast(meter.bridge.meter_ptr));\n\n    // // Collect metrics directly\n    // const metrics = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer cleanupMetrics(allocator, metrics);\n\n    // // We should have metrics for each instrument that recorded data\n    // // Note: Empty instruments might be skipped, so we check for >= expected minimums\n    // try testing.expect(metrics.len >= 5);\n\n    // Temporary: Just verify instruments were created successfully\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable metric verification once collection is implemented\n    // // Verify each metric has proper structure\n    // for (metrics) |metric| {\n    //     try testing.expect(metric.name.len > 0);\n    //     try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n    //     try testing.expect(api.InstrumentationScope.eql(metric.scope, scope));\n\n    //     // Test attributes are empty (MVP state)\n    //     // TODO: This test should break when attributes are implemented\n    //     for (metric.data_points) |data_point| {\n    //         try testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 0), data_point.attributes.len);\n    //     }\n    // }\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter data collection through processor pipeline\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n    // Processor takes ownership of this memory\n\n    // Create processor (heap-allocated)\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.ManualReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.ManualReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter());\n\n    // Register reader (provider takes ownership)\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Get meter\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create instruments and record data\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"http.requests\", \"HTTP requests\", \"requests\", null);\n    const histogram = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"http.duration\", \"HTTP duration\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    counter.add(ctx, 3, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 12.5, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Force collection via processor (pull model)\n    reader.collect();\n\n    // Verify metrics were exported\n    try testing.expect(mock_exporter.metricCount() > 0);\n\n    // Check that we have the expected metric types\n    var found_counter = false;\n    var found_histogram = false;\n\n    for (0..mock_exporter.metricCount()) |i| {\n        if (mock_exporter.getMetric(i)) |metric| {\n            if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"http.requests\")) {\n                found_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"http.duration\")) {\n                found_histogram = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.histogram, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    try testing.expect(found_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_histogram);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter shutdown behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create counter and record data before shutdown\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance for direct access\n    const basic_meter: *sdk.Meter = @ptrCast(@alignCast(meter.bridge.meter_ptr));\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable once collection is implemented at reader level\n    // // Verify data exists before shutdown\n    // const metrics_before = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer cleanupMetrics(allocator, metrics_before);\n    // try testing.expect(metrics_before.len > 0);\n\n    // Shutdown the meter\n    basic_meter.shutdown();\n    try testing.expect(basic_meter.is_shutdown.load(.unordered));\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable once collection is implemented at reader level\n    // // Test that collectMetrics still works after shutdown (data preserved)\n    // const metrics_after = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer cleanupMetrics(allocator, metrics_after);\n    // try testing.expect(metrics_after.len > 0);\n\n    // TODO: The following test should work when shutdown behavior is fully implemented\n    // Currently commented out as it may not be implemented yet\n    //\n    // // Try to record new data after shutdown - should be ignored\n    // counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    //\n    // // Verify the counter value didn't change\n    // const metrics_final = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer allocator.free(metrics_final);\n    // // Should still have same data as before the post-shutdown recording\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeterProvider flush behavior\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.ManualReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.ManualReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter());\n\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Test successful flush\n    const result = provider.forceFlush(1000);\n    try testing.expectEqual(api.common.FlushResult.success, result);\n\n    // Test flush with failure\n    mock_exporter.flush_result = .failure;\n    const result2 = provider.forceFlush(1000);\n    try testing.expectEqual(api.common.FlushResult.failure, result2);\n}\n\ntest \"BasicMeter comprehensive instrument test with attributes\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Test custom histogram boundaries (for future use)\n    _ = [_]f64{ 0.0, 1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 50.0 };\n\n    // Create all instrument types with different value types\n    const counter_i64 = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.i64\", \"Test i64 counter\", \"ops\", null);\n    const counter_f64 = try meter.createCounter(f64, \"test.counter.f64\", \"Test f64 counter\", \"seconds\", null);\n    const updown_i64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.updown.i64\", \"Test i64 updown\", \"items\", null);\n    const updown_f64 = try meter.createUpDownCounter(f64, \"test.updown.f64\", \"Test f64 updown\", \"temperature\", null);\n    const gauge_i64 = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.i64\", \"Test i64 gauge\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const gauge_f64 = try meter.createGauge(f64, \"test.gauge.f64\", \"Test f64 gauge\", \"ratio\", null);\n    const histogram_i64 = try meter.createHistogram(i64, \"test.histogram.i64\", \"Test i64 histogram\", \"count\", null);\n    const histogram_f64 = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.f64\", \"Test f64 histogram\", \"latency\", null);\n\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    // TODO: Test with attributes when attribute support is implemented\n    // This test should break when attributes are added to remind us to update it\n    // const attributes = [_]AttributeKeyValue{\n    //     .{ .key = \"method\", .value = .{ .string = \"GET\" } },\n    //     .{ .key = \"status\", .value = .{ .int = 200 } },\n    // };\n\n    // Record various measurements\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_i64.add(ctx, 25, &empty_attributes); // Total: 40\n    counter_f64.add(ctx, 3.14, &empty_attributes);\n    counter_f64.add(ctx, 2.86, &empty_attributes); // Total: 6.0\n\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_i64.add(ctx, -3, &empty_attributes); // Total: 7\n    updown_f64.add(ctx, 5.5, &empty_attributes);\n    updown_f64.add(ctx, -1.5, &empty_attributes); // Total: 4.0\n\n    gauge_i64.record(ctx, 1024, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge_i64.record(ctx, 2048, &empty_attributes); // Last: 2048\n    gauge_f64.record(ctx, 0.85, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge_f64.record(ctx, 0.92, &empty_attributes); // Last: 0.92\n\n    // Test histogram with various values to hit different buckets\n    histogram_i64.record(ctx, 2, &empty_attributes); // bucket 1 (1-5)\n    histogram_i64.record(ctx, 7, &empty_attributes); // bucket 2 (5-10)\n    histogram_i64.record(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes); // bucket 3 (10-50)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 0.5, &empty_attributes); // bucket 0 (0-1)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 3.2, &empty_attributes); // bucket 1 (1-5)\n    histogram_f64.record(ctx, 25.0, &empty_attributes); // bucket 3 (10-50)\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable once collection is implemented at reader level\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance for direct collectMetrics call\n    // const basic_meter: *sdk.Meter = @ptrCast(@alignCast(meter.bridge.meter_ptr));\n\n    // // Collect metrics\n    // const metrics = try basic_meter.collectMetrics(allocator);\n    // defer cleanupMetrics(allocator, metrics);\n\n    // Temporary: Just verify instruments were created successfully\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable once collection is implemented\n    // // Should have metrics for all instruments with recorded data\n    // try testing.expect(metrics.len >= 8);\n\n    // TODO: Phase 1b - Re-enable metric verification once collection is implemented\n    // // Verify comprehensive metric properties\n    // var counters_found: u32 = 0;\n    // var gauges_found: u32 = 0;\n    // var histograms_found: u32 = 0;\n\n    // for (metrics) |metric| {\n    //     // Verify common properties\n {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create instrument before shutdown - should be normal SDK instrument\n    const counter_before = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.before\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    try testing.expect(counter_before == .bridge); // Should be bridge to SDK instrument\n\n    // Get the BasicMeter instance to shutdown directly\n    const basic_meter: *sdk.Meter = @ptrCast(@alignCast(meter.bridge.meter_ptr));\n    basic_meter.shutdown();\n\n    // Create instruments after shutdown - should be noop instruments\n    const counter_after = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter.after\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const updown_after = try meter.createUpDownCounter(f64, \"test.updown.after\", \"Test updown\", \"bytes\", null);\n    const gauge_after = try meter.createGauge(i64, \"test.gauge.after\", \"Test gauge\", \"items\", null);\n    const histogram_after = try meter.createHistogram(f64, \"test.histogram.after\", \"Test histogram\", \"ms\", null);\n\n    // All instruments created after shutdown should be noop\n    try testing.expect(counter_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(updown_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(gauge_after == .noop);\n    try testing.expect(histogram_after == .noop);\n\n    // Verify noop instruments have the correct names\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.counter.after\", counter_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.updown.after\", updown_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.gauge.after\", gauge_after.noop);\n    try testing.expectEqualStrings(\"test.histogram.after\", histogram_after.noop);\n}\n\ntest \"PeriodicReader with multiple instruments\" {\n    const testing = std.testing;\n    const allocator = testing.allocator;\n\n    const resource = try sdk.ResourceBuilder.init(allocator)\n        .withDefaults()\n        .finish(allocator);\n\n    var provider = sdk.MeterProvider.init(allocator, resource);\n    defer provider.deinit();\n\n    // Create mock exporter\n    const mock_exporter = try allocator.create(MockMetricExporter);\n    mock_exporter.* = MockMetricExporter.init(allocator);\n    // Processor takes ownership of this memory\n\n    // Create periodic reader with 100ms interval\n    const reader = try allocator.create(sdk.PeriodicReader);\n    reader.* = sdk.PeriodicReader.init(allocator, mock_exporter.metricExporter(), 100);\n\n    // Register reader (provider takes ownership)\n    try provider.registerProcessor(reader.reader());\n\n    // Get meter\n    const scope = try api.InstrumentationScope.initSimple(\"test.meter\", \"1.0.0\");\n    var meter = try provider.getMeterWithScope(scope);\n\n    // Create regular instruments\n    const counter = try meter.createCounter(i64, \"test.counter\", \"Test counter\", \"requests\", null);\n    const gauge = try meter.createGauge(f64, \"test.gauge\", \"Test gauge\", \"ratio\", null);\n    const histogram = try meter.createHistogram(i64, \"test.histogram\", \"Test histogram\", \"count\", null);\n\n    // Create observable instruments\n    const observable_counter = try meter.createObservableCounter(i64, \"test.observable.counter\", \"Test observable counter\", \"requests\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_gauge = try meter.createObservableGauge(f64, \"test.observable.gauge\", \"Test observable gauge\", \"ratio\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n    const observable_updown = try meter.createObservableUpDownCounter(i64, \"test.observable.updown\", \"Test observable updown\", \"items\", null, &[_]api.metrics.TypeErasedCallback{});\n\n    // Record some data with regular instruments\n    const ctx = api.Context.init(allocator);\n    const empty_attributes = [_]api.AttributeKeyValue{};\n\n    counter.add(ctx, 10, &empty_attributes);\n    counter.add(ctx, 5, &empty_attributes);\n    gauge.record(ctx, 0.75, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 15, &empty_attributes);\n    histogram.record(ctx, 25, &empty_attributes);\n\n    // Setup callbacks for observable instruments\n    const counter_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(42, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const gauge_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(f64)) void {\n            result.observe(0.95, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n    const updown_callback = struct {\n        fn callback(result: *api.metrics.ObservableResult(i64)) void {\n            result.observe(100, &empty_attributes, null) catch {};\n        }\n    }.callback;\n\n    // Register the callbacks\n    const counter_handle = try observable_counter.registerCallbackNoState(counter_callback);\n    const gauge_handle = try observable_gauge.registerCallbackNoState(gauge_callback);\n    const updown_handle = try observable_updown.registerCallbackNoState(updown_callback);\n\n    defer {\n        counter_handle.unregister();\n        gauge_handle.unregister();\n        updown_handle.unregister();\n    }\n\n    // Wait for periodic collection to happen (simulate time passing)\n    std.time.sleep(200 * std.time.ns_per_ms);\n\n    // Force a final collection\n    reader.collect();\n\n    // Verify metrics were exported\n    try testing.expect(mock_exporter.metricCount() > 0);\n\n    // Check that we have the expected metric types\n    var found_counter = false;\n    var found_gauge = false;\n    var found_histogram = false;\n    var found_observable_counter = false;\n    var found_observable_gauge = false;\n    var found_observable_updown = false;\n\n    std.debug.print(\"\\nDEBUG: Total metrics exported: {}\\n\", .{mock_exporter.metricCount()});\n    for (0..mock_exporter.metricCount()) |i| {\n        if (mock_exporter.getMetric(i)) |metric| {\n            std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: Metric {}: name='{s}', type={}, data_points={}\\n\", .{ i, metric.name, metric.type, metric.data_points.len });\n            if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.counter\")) {\n                found_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.gauge\")) {\n                found_gauge = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.gauge, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.histogram\")) {\n                found_histogram = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.histogram, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.counter\")) {\n                found_observable_counter = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.gauge\")) {\n                found_observable_gauge = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.gauge, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, metric.name, \"test.observable.updown\")) {\n                found_observable_updown = true;\n                try testing.expectEqual(sdk.MetricType.sum, metric.type);\n                try testing.expect(metric.data_points.len > 0);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: found_counter={}, found_gauge={}, found_histogram={}\\n\", .{ found_counter, found_gauge, found_histogram });\n    std.debug.print(\"DEBUG: found_observable_counter={}, found_observable_gauge={}, found_observable_updown={}\\n\", .{ found_observable_counter, found_observable_gauge, found_observable_updown });\n\n    try testing.expect(found_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_gauge);\n    try testing.expect(found_histogram);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_counter);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_gauge);\n    try testing.expect(found_observable_updown);\n}\n",
